revision.text:
{{神社 |名称 = 志理太乎宜神社 |画像 = {{画像募集中|cat=賀茂郡}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=14|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=250|frame-height=250|coord={{coord|34|48|8.3|N|139|3|33.8|E}}}} |所在地 = [[静岡県]][[賀茂郡]][[東伊豆町]]白田168 |位置 = {{ウィキ座標2段度分秒|34|48|8.3|N|139|3|33.8|E|display=inline,title}} |祭神 = 志理太乎宜命、[[五十猛命]] |社格 = 旧[[村社]]、[[式内社]]論社 |創建 = |本殿 = |別名 = 白田来(浜)神社、(白田)来宮神社 |札所等 = |例祭 = 10月24日-25日 |神事 = }} '''志理太乎宜神社'''（しりたおぎじんじゃ）は、[[静岡県]][[賀茂郡]][[東伊豆町]]白田（しらだ）にある[[神社]]<ref name="jinjacho">[http://www.shizuoka-jinjacho.or.jp/shokai/jinja.php?id=4401004 志理太乎宜神社] - 静岡県神社庁</ref>。江戸時代には'''来宮神社'''（きのみやじんじゃ）と呼ばれており、現在でもこの名が別称として併用され、鳥居の扁額などにもこの名が用いられている<ref name="kotobank">[https://kotobank.jp/word/白田村-3062457 白田村とは] - コトバンク</ref><ref name="engi-chosa">[http://engishiki.org/izu/bun/iz140133-02.html 志理太乎宜神社] - 延喜式神社の調査</ref>。
== 概要 == [[片瀬白田駅]]の西方至近、[[国道135号]]沿いに鎮座している。
[[三宅島]]の[[椎取神社]]と共に、[[三島大明神]]（[[事代主神]]）の王子の一人・志理太(乎)宜命（しりた(お)き[ぎ]のみこと）を祀る[[式内社]]である'''志理太(乎)宜神社'''（しりた(お)き[ぎ]じんじゃ）の論社となっているが、同時期に創建されたと考えられる北隣・片瀬（かたせ）地区の[[片菅神社 (東伊豆町)|片菅神社]]（かたすげじんじゃ）が、同じく三宅島の三島大明神（事代主神）の別の王子を祀る式内社に比定されている[[片菅神社 (三宅村)|片菅神社]]の分社的性格を有しているため、この志理太乎宜神社も、元々はそうした噴火を避けた避難的分社・遥拝所だった可能性がある<ref name="engi-chosa"/><ref name="engi-chosa2">[http://engishiki.org/izu/bun/iz140116-04.html 片菅神社] - 延喜式神社の調査</ref><ref name="choshi"/>。
創建は不詳だが、[[康永]]2年（[[1343年]]）再建の記録が『南豆風土誌』に残っている<ref name="choshi">[https://www.town.higashiizu.shizuoka.jp/bg/life/upload/kyo_higashiizutyousi_30mini_6.pdf 『東伊豆町誌』第6章 p.360] - 東伊豆町</ref>。また[[貞和]]2年（[[1346年]]）の上梁文などには'''白田来(浜)神社'''（しらだき(はま)じんじゃ）とある（『増訂豆州志稿』）<ref name="kotobank"/>。
また、旧社名や配祀されている祭神・[[五十猛命]]からも分かるように、江戸時代には[[キノミヤ信仰]]を有する来宮神社としての性格が顕著であり、現在も[[河津町]]の[[川津来宮神社]]（[[杉桙別命神社]]）と同じく、「[[河津の鳥精進酒精進|鳥精進・酒精進]]」の文化を有している<ref name="engi-chosa"/><ref name="yuisho">境内由緒書</ref>。
== 祭神 == * 志理太乎宜命（しりたおぎのみこと） * [[五十猛命]]

創建は不詳だが、[[康永]]2年（[[1343年]]）再建の記録が『南豆風土誌』に残っている<ref name="choshi">[https://www.town.higashiizu.shizuoka.jp/bg/life/upload/kyo_higashiizutyousi_30mini_6.pdf 『東伊豆町誌』第6章 p.360] - 東伊豆町</ref>。また[[貞和]]2年（[[1346年]]）の上梁文などには'''白田来(浜)神社'''（しらだき(はま)じんじゃ）とある（『増訂豆州志稿』）<ref name="kotobank"/>。
また、旧社名や配祀されている祭神・[[五十猛命]]からも分かるように、江戸時代には[[キノミヤ信仰]]を有する来宮神社としての性格が顕著であり、現在も[[河津町]]の[[川津来宮神社]]（[[杉桙別命神社]]）と同じく、「[[河津の鳥精進酒精進|鳥精進・酒精進]]」の文化を有している<ref name="engi-chosa"/><ref name="yuisho">境内由緒書</ref>。

{{デフォルトソート:しりたおきしんしや}} [[Category:静岡県の神社]] [[Category:東伊豆町の歴史]] [[Category:東伊豆町の建築物]] [[Category:伊豆国の式内社]]

{{神社 |名称 = 志理太乎宜神社 |画像 = {{画像募集中|cat=賀茂郡}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=14|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=250|frame-height=250|coord={{coord|34|48|8.3|N|139|3|33.8|E}}}} |所在地 = [[静岡県]][[賀茂郡]][[東伊豆町]]白田168 |位置 = {{ウィキ座標2段度分秒|34|48|8.3|N|139|3|33.8|E|display=inline,title}} |祭神 = 志理太乎宜命、[[五十猛命]] |社格 = 旧[[村社]]、[[式内社]]論社 |創建 = |本殿 = |別名 = 白田来(浜)神社、(白田)来宮神社 |札所等 = |例祭 = 10月24日-25日 |神事 = }} '''志理太乎宜神社'''（しりたおぎじんじゃ）は、[[静岡県]][[賀茂郡]][[東伊豆町]]白田（しらだ）にある[[神社]]<ref name="jinjacho">[http://www.shizuoka-jinjacho.or.jp/shokai/jinja.php?id=4401004 志理太乎宜神社] - 静岡県神社庁</ref>。江戸時代には'''来宮神社'''（きのみやじんじゃ）と呼ばれており、現在でもこの名が別称として併用され、鳥居の扁額などにもこの名が用いられている<ref name="kotobank">[https://kotobank.jp/word/白田村-3062457 白田村とは] - コトバンク</ref><ref name="engi-chosa">[http://engishiki.org/izu/bun/iz140133-02.html 志理太乎宜神社] - 延喜式神社の調査</ref>。
== 概要 == [[片瀬白田駅]]の西方至近、[[国道135号]]沿いに鎮座している。
[[三宅島]]の[[椎取神社]]と共に、[[三島大明神]]（[[事代主神]]）の王子の一人・志理太(乎)宜命（しりた(お)き[ぎ]のみこと）を祀る[[式内社]]である'''志理太(乎)宜神社'''（しりた(お)き[ぎ]じんじゃ）の論社となっているが、同時期に創建されたと考えられる北隣りの片瀬（かたせ）地区の[[片菅神社 (東伊豆町)|片菅神社]]（かたすげじんじゃ）もまた、同じく三宅島の三島大明神（事代主神）の別の王子を祀る式内社の論社として、三宅島の[[片菅神社]]と競合する分社的性格を有しているため、これらの神社は、元々はそうした三宅島の神社が噴火を避けて移転してきた避難的分社・遥拝所だった可能性がある<ref name="engi-chosa"/><ref name="engi-chosa2">[http://engishiki.org/izu/bun/iz140116-04.html 片菅神社] - 延喜式神社の調査</ref><ref name="choshi"/>。
創建は不詳だが、[[康永]]2年（[[1343年]]）再建の記録が『南豆風土誌』に残っている<ref name="choshi">[https://www.town.higashiizu.shizuoka.jp/bg/life/upload/kyo_higashiizutyousi_30mini_6.pdf 『東伊豆町誌』第6章 p.360] - 東伊豆町</ref>。また[[貞和]]2年（[[1346年]]）の上梁文などには'''白田来(浜)神社'''（しらだき(はま)じんじゃ）とある（『増訂豆州志稿』）<ref name="kotobank"/>。
また、旧社名や配祀されている祭神・[[五十猛命]]からも分かるように、江戸時代には[[キノミヤ信仰]]を有する来宮神社としての性格が顕著であり、現在も[[河津町]]の[[川津来宮神社]]（[[杉桙別命神社]]）と同じく、「[[河津の鳥精進酒精進|鳥精進・酒精進]]」の文化を有している<ref name="engi-chosa"/><ref name="yuisho">境内由緒書</ref><ref name="ss">[https://www.at-s.com/facilities/article/view/temple/137787.html 来宮神社 (東伊豆町)] - 静岡新聞</ref>。

{{神社 |名称 = 志理太乎宜神社 |画像 = {{画像募集中|cat=賀茂郡}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=14|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=250|frame-height=250|coord={{coord|34|48|8.3|N|139|3|33.8|E}}}} |所在地 = [[静岡県]][[賀茂郡]][[東伊豆町]]白田168 |位置 = {{ウィキ座標2段度分秒|34|48|8.3|N|139|3|33.8|E|display=inline,title}} |祭神 = 志理太乎宜命、[[五十猛命]] |社格 = 旧[[村社]]、[[式内社]]論社 |創建 = |本殿 = |別名 = 白田来(浜)神社、(白田)来宮神社 |札所等 = |例祭 = 10月24日-25日 |神事 = }} '''志理太乎宜神社'''（しりたおぎじんじゃ）は、[[静岡県]][[賀茂郡]][[東伊豆町]]白田（しらだ）にある[[神社]]<ref name="jinjacho">[http://www.shizuoka-jinjacho.or.jp/shokai/jinja.php?id=4401004 志理太乎宜神社] - 静岡県神社庁</ref>。江戸時代には'''来宮神社'''（きのみやじんじゃ）と呼ばれており、現在でもこの名が別称として併用され、鳥居の扁額などにもこの名が用いられている<ref name="kotobank">[https://kotobank.jp/word/白田村-3062457 白田村とは] - コトバンク</ref><ref name="engi-chosa">[http://engishiki.org/izu/bun/iz140133-02.html 志理太乎宜神社] - 延喜式神社の調査</ref>。
== 概要 == [[片瀬白田駅]]の西方至近、[[国道135号]]沿いに鎮座している。
[[三宅島]]の[[椎取神社]]と共に、[[三島大明神]]（[[事代主神]]）の王子の一人・志理太(乎)宜命（しりた(お)き[ぎ]のみこと）を祀る[[式内社]]である'''志理太(乎)宜神社'''（しりた(お)き[ぎ]じんじゃ）の論社となっているが、同時期に創建されたと考えられる北隣りの片瀬（かたせ）地区の[[片菅神社 (東伊豆町)|片菅神社]]（かたすげじんじゃ）もまた、同じく三宅島の三島大明神（事代主神）の別の王子を祀る式内社の論社として、三宅島の[[片菅神社]]と競合する分社的性格を有しているため、これらの神社は、元々はそうした三宅島の神社が噴火を避けて移転してきた避難的分社・遥拝所だった可能性がある<ref name="engi-chosa"/><ref name="engi-chosa2">[http://engishiki.org/izu/bun/iz140116-04.html 片菅神社] - 延喜式神社の調査</ref><ref name="choshi"/>。
創建は不詳だが、[[康永]]2年（[[1343年]]）再建の記録が『南豆風土誌』に残っている<ref name="choshi">[https://www.town.higashiizu.shizuoka.jp/bg/life/upload/kyo_higashiizutyousi_30mini_6.pdf 『東伊豆町誌』第6章 p.360] - 東伊豆町</ref>。また[[貞和]]2年（[[1346年]]）の上梁文などには'''白田来(浜)神社'''（しらだき(はま)じんじゃ）とある（『増訂豆州志稿』）<ref name="kotobank"/>。
また、旧社名や配祀されている祭神・[[五十猛命]]からも分かるように、江戸時代には[[キノミヤ信仰]]を有する来宮神社としての性格も顕著であり、現在も[[河津町]]の[[川津来宮神社]]（[[杉桙別命神社]]）と同じく、「[[河津の鳥精進酒精進|鳥精進・酒精進]]」の文化を有している<ref name="engi-chosa"/><ref name="yuisho">境内由緒書</ref><ref name="ss">[https://www.at-s.com/facilities/article/view/temple/137787.html 来宮神社 (東伊豆町)] - 静岡新聞</ref>。
== 祭神 == * 志理太乎宜命（しりたおぎのみこと） * [[五十猛命]]
== 脚注 == {{Reflist}}
== 外部リンク == * [http://www.shizuoka-jinjacho.or.jp/shokai/jinja.php?id=4401004 志理太乎宜神社] - 静岡県神社庁
{{デフォルトソート:しりたおきしんしや}} [[Category:静岡県の神社]] [[Category:東伊豆町の歴史]] [[Category:東伊豆町の建築物]] [[Category:伊豆国の式内社]]

乎宜神社|coordinates= {{coord2|34|48|8.3|N|139|3|33.8|E|}}|position=left|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} }} '''志理太乎宜神社'''（しりたおぎじんじゃ）は、[[静岡県]][[賀茂郡]][[東伊豆町]]白田（しらだ）にある[[神社]][http://www.shizuoka-jinjacho.or.jp/shokai/jinja.php?id=4401004 志理太乎宜神社] - 静岡県神社庁。江戸時代には'''来宮神社'''（きのみやじんじゃ）と呼ばれており、現在でもこの名が別称として併用され、鳥居の扁額などにもこの名が用いられている[https://kotobank.jp/word/白田村-3062457 白田村とは] - コトバンク[http://engishiki.org/izu/bun/iz140133-02.html 志理太乎宜神社] - 延喜式神社の調査。 == 概要 == [[片瀬白田駅]]の西方至近、[[国道135号]]沿いに鎮座している。 [[三宅島]]の[[椎取神社]]と共に、[[三島大明神]]（[[事代主神]]）の王子の一人・志理太(乎)宜命（しりた(お)き[ぎ]のみこと）を祀る[[式内社]]である'''志理太(乎)宜神社'''（しりた(お)き[ぎ]じんじゃ）の論社となっているが、同時期に創建されたと考えられる北隣りの片瀬（かたせ）地区の[[片菅神社 (東伊豆町)|片菅神社]]（かたすげじんじゃ）もまた、同じく三宅島の三島大明神（事代主神）の別の王子を祀る式内社の論社として、三宅島の[[片菅神社]]と競合する分社的性格を有しているため、これらの神社は、元々はそうした三宅島の神社が噴火を避けて移転してきた避難的分社・遥拝所だった可能性がある[http://engishiki.org/izu/bun/iz140116-04.html 片菅神社] - 延喜式神社の調査。 創建は不詳だが、[[康永]]2年（[[1343年]]）再建の記録が『南豆風土誌』に残っている[https://www.town.higashiizu.shizuoka.jp/bg/life/upload/kyo_higashiizutyousi_30mini_6.pdf 『東伊豆町誌』第6章 p.360] - 東伊豆町。また[[貞和]]2年（[[1346年]]）の上梁文などには'''白田来(浜)神社'''（しらだき(はま)じんじゃ）とある（『増訂豆州志稿』）。 また、旧社名や配祀されている祭神・[[五十猛命]]からも分かるように、江戸時代には[[キノミヤ信仰]]を有する来宮神社としての性格も顕著であり、現在も[[河津町]]の[[川津来宮神社]]（[[杉桙別命神社]]）と同じく、「[[河津の鳥精進酒精進|鳥精進・酒精進]]」の文化を有している境内由緒書[https://www.at-s.com/facilities/article/view/temple/137787.html 来宮神社 (東伊豆町)] - 静岡新聞。 == 祭神 == * 志理太乎宜命（しりたおぎのみこと） * [[五十猛命]] == 脚注 == {{Reflist}} == 外部リンク == * [http://www.shizuoka-jinjacho.or.jp/shokai/jinja.php?id=4401004 志理太乎宜神社] - 静岡県神社庁 {{デフォルトソート:しりたおきしんしや}} [[Category:静岡県の神社]] [[Category:東伊豆町の歴史]] [[Category:東伊豆町の建築物]] [[Category:伊豆国の式内社]]

{{nihongo|'''Shiritaogi Shrine'''|志理太乎宜神社}} is a [[shinto shrine]] in the [[Engishiki Jinmyōchō]]. It is located in [[Izu Province]].

{{nihongo|'''Shiritaogi Shrine'''|志理太乎宜神社}} is a [[shinto shrine]] in the [[Engishiki Jinmyōchō]]. It is located in [[Izu Province]].

{{nihongo|'''Shiritaogi Shrine'''|志理太乎宜神社}} is a [[shinto shrine]] in the [[Engishiki Jinmyōchō]]. It is located in [[Izu Province]].

{{nihongo|'''Shiritaogi Shrine'''|志理太乎宜神社}} is a [[shinto shrine]] in the [[Engishiki Jinmyōchō]]. It is located in [[Izu Province]].

{{nihongo|'''Shiritaogi Shrine'''|志理太乎宜神社}} is a [[shinto shrine]] in the [[Engishiki Jinmyōchō]]. It is located in [[Izu Province]].

{{nihongo|'''Shiritaogi Shrine'''|志理太乎宜神社}} is a [[shinto shrine]] in the [[Engishiki Jinmyōchō]]. It is located in [[Izu Province]].

{{nihongo|'''Shiritaogi Shrine'''|志理太乎宜神社}} is a [[shinto shrine]] in the [[Engishiki Jinmyōchō]]. It is located in [[Izu Province]].

revision.comment: 6 revisions imported
revision.text:
{{神社 |名称 = 志理太乎宜神社 |画像 = {{画像募集中|cat=賀茂郡}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=14|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=250|frame-height=250|coord={{coord|34|48|8.3|N|139|3|33.8|E}}}} |所在地 = [[静岡県]][[賀茂郡]][[東伊豆町]]白田168 |位置 = {{ウィキ座標2段度分秒|34|48|8.3|N|139|3|33.8|E|display=inline,title}} |祭神 = 志理太乎宜命、[[五十猛命]] |社格 = 旧[[村社]]、[[式内社]]論社 |創建 = |本殿 = |別名 = 白田来(浜)神社、(白田)来宮神社 |札所等 = |例祭 = 10月24日-25日 |神事 = |地図 = {{Location map|Japan Shizuoka|label=志理太
乎宜神社|coordinates= {{coord2|34|48|8.3|N|139|3|33.8|E|}}|position=left|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} }} '''志理太乎宜神社'''（しりたおぎじんじゃ）は、[[静岡県]][[賀茂郡]][[東伊豆町]]白田（しらだ）にある[[神社]][http://www.shizuoka-jinjacho.or.jp/shokai/jinja.php?id=4401004 志理太乎宜神社] - 静岡県神社庁。江戸時代には'''来宮神社'''（きのみやじんじゃ）と呼ばれており、現在でもこの名が別称として併用され、鳥居の扁額などにもこの名が用いられている[https://kotobank.jp/word/白田村-3062457 白田村とは] - コトバンク[http://engishiki.org/izu/bun/iz140133-02.html 志理太乎宜神社] - 延喜式神社の調査。 == 概要 == [[片瀬白田駅]]の西方至近、[[国道135号]]沿いに鎮座している。 [[三宅島]]の[[椎取神社]]と共に、[[三島大明神]]（[[事代主神]]）の王子の一人・志理太(乎)宜命（しりた(お)き[ぎ]のみこと）を祀る[[式内社]]である'''志理太(乎)宜神社'''（しりた(お)き[ぎ]じんじゃ）の論社となっているが、同時期に創建されたと考えられる北隣りの片瀬（かたせ）地区の[[片菅神社 (東伊豆町)|片菅神社]]（かたすげじんじゃ）もまた、同じく三宅島の三島大明神（事代主神）の別の王子を祀る式内社の論社として、三宅島の[[片菅神社]]と競合する分社的性格を有しているため、これらの神社は、元々はそうした三宅島の神社が噴火を避けて移転してきた避難的分社・遥拝所だった可能性がある[http://engishiki.org/izu/bun/iz140116-04.html 片菅神社] - 延喜式神社の調査。 創建は不詳だが、[[康永]]2年（[[1343年]]）再建の記録が『南豆風土誌』に残っている[https://www.town.higashiizu.shizuoka.jp/bg/life/upload/kyo_higashiizutyousi_30mini_6.pdf 『東伊豆町誌』第6章 p.360] - 東伊豆町。また[[貞和]]2年（[[1346年]]）の上梁文などには'''白田来(浜)神社'''（しらだき(はま)じんじゃ）とある（『増訂豆州志稿』）。 また、旧社名や配祀されている祭神・[[五十猛命]]からも分かるように、江戸時代には[[キノミヤ信仰]]を有する来宮神社としての性格も顕著であり、現在も[[河津町]]の[[川津来宮神社]]（[[杉桙別命神社]]）と同じく、「[[河津の鳥精進酒精進|鳥精進・酒精進]]」の文化を有している境内由緒書[https://www.at-s.com/facilities/article/view/temple/137787.html 来宮神社 (東伊豆町)] - 静岡新聞。 == 祭神 == * 志理太乎宜命（しりたおぎのみこと） * [[五十猛命]] == 脚注 == {{Reflist}} == 外部リンク == * [http://www.shizuoka-jinjacho.or.jp/shokai/jinja.php?id=4401004 志理太乎宜神社] - 静岡県神社庁 {{デフォルトソート:しりたおきしんしや}} [[Category:静岡県の神社]] [[Category:東伊豆町の歴史]] [[Category:東伊豆町の建築物]] [[Category:伊豆国の式内社]]

revision.text:
{{神社 |名称 = 志理太乎宜神社 |画像 = {{画像募集中|cat=賀茂郡}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=14|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=250|frame-height=250|coord={{coord|34|48|8.3|N|139|3|33.8|E}}}} |所在地 = [[静岡県]][[賀茂郡]][[東伊豆町]]白田168 |位置 = {{ウィキ座標2段度分秒|34|48|8.3|N|139|3|33.8|E|display=inline,title}} |祭神 = 志理太乎宜命、[[五十猛命]] |社格 = 旧[[村社]]、[[式内社]]論社 |創建 = |本殿 = |別名 = 白田来(浜)神社、(白田)来宮神社 |札所等 = |例祭 = 10月24日-25日 |神事 = |地図 = {{Location map|Japan Shizuoka|label=志理太
乎宜神社|coordinates= {{coord2|34|48|8.3|N|139|3|33.8|E|}}|position=left|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} }} '''志理太乎宜神社'''（しりたおぎじんじゃ）は、[[静岡県]][[賀茂郡]][[東伊豆町]]白田（しらだ）にある[[神社]][http://www.shizuoka-jinjacho.or.jp/shokai/jinja.php?id=4401004 志理太乎宜神社] - 静岡県神社庁。江戸時代には'''来宮神社'''（きのみやじんじゃ）と呼ばれており、現在でもこの名が別称として併用され、鳥居の扁額などにもこの名が用いられている[https://kotobank.jp/word/白田村-3062457 白田村とは] - コトバンク[http://engishiki.org/izu/bun/iz140133-02.html 志理太乎宜神社] - 延喜式神社の調査。 == 概要 == [[片瀬白田駅]]の西方至近、[[国道135号]]沿いに鎮座している。 [[三宅島]]の[[椎取神社]]と共に、[[三島大明神]]（[[事代主神]]）の王子の一人・志理太(乎)宜命（しりた(お)き[ぎ]のみこと）を祀る[[式内社]]である'''志理太(乎)宜神社'''（しりた(お)き[ぎ]じんじゃ）の論社となっているが、同時期に創建されたと考えられる北隣りの片瀬（かたせ）地区の[[片菅神社 (東伊豆町)|片菅神社]]（かたすげじんじゃ）もまた、同じく三宅島の三島大明神（事代主神）の別の王子を祀る式内社の論社として、三宅島の[[片菅神社]]と競合する分社的性格を有しているため、これらの神社は、元々はそうした三宅島の神社が噴火を避けて移転してきた避難的分社・遥拝所だった可能性がある[http://engishiki.org/izu/bun/iz140116-04.html 片菅神社] - 延喜式神社の調査。 創建は不詳だが、[[康永]]2年（[[1343年]]）再建の記録が『南豆風土誌』に残っている[https://www.town.higashiizu.shizuoka.jp/bg/life/upload/kyo_higashiizutyousi_30mini_6.pdf 『東伊豆町誌』第6章 p.360] - 東伊豆町。また[[貞和]]2年（[[1346年]]）の上梁文などには'''白田来(浜)神社'''（しらだき(はま)じんじゃ）とある（『増訂豆州志稿』）。 また、旧社名や配祀されている祭神・[[五十猛命]]からも分かるように、江戸時代には[[キノミヤ信仰]]を有する来宮神社としての性格も顕著であり、現在も[[河津町]]の[[川津来宮神社]]（[[杉桙別命神社]]）と同じく、「[[河津の鳥精進酒精進|鳥精進・酒精進]]」の文化を有している境内由緒書[https://www.at-s.com/facilities/article/view/temple/137787.html 来宮神社 (東伊豆町)] - 静岡新聞。 == 祭神 == * 志理太乎宜命（しりたおぎのみこと） * [[五十猛命]] == 脚注 == {{Reflist}} == 外部リンク == * [http://www.shizuoka-jinjacho.or.jp/shokai/jinja.php?id=4401004 志理太乎宜神社] - 静岡県神社庁 {{デフォルトソート:しりたおきしんしや}} [[Category:静岡県の神社]] [[Category:東伊豆町の歴史]] [[Category:東伊豆町の建築物]] [[Category:伊豆国の式内社]]

乎宜神社|coordinates= {{coord2|34|48|8.3|N|139|3|33.8|E|}}|position=left|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} }} '''志理太乎宜神社'''（しりたおぎじんじゃ）は、[[静岡県]][[賀茂郡]][[東伊豆町]]白田（しらだ）にある[[神社]][http://www.shizuoka-jinjacho.or.jp/shokai/jinja.php?id=4401004 志理太乎宜神社] - 静岡県神社庁。江戸時代には'''来宮神社'''（きのみやじんじゃ）と呼ばれており、現在でもこの名が別称として併用され、鳥居の扁額などにもこの名が用いられている[https://kotobank.jp/word/白田村-3062457 白田村とは] - コトバンク[http://engishiki.org/izu/bun/iz140133-02.html 志理太乎宜神社] - 延喜式神社の調査。 == 概要 == [[片瀬白田駅]]の西方至近、[[国道135号]]沿いに鎮座している。 [[三宅島]]の[[椎取神社]]と共に、[[三島大明神]]（[[事代主神]]）の王子の一人・志理太(乎)宜命（しりた(お)き[ぎ]のみこと）を祀る[[式内社]]である'''志理太(乎)宜神社'''（しりた(お)き[ぎ]じんじゃ）の論社となっているが、同時期に創建されたと考えられる北隣りの片瀬（かたせ）地区の[[片菅神社 (東伊豆町)|片菅神社]]（かたすげじんじゃ）もまた、同じく三宅島の三島大明神（事代主神）の別の王子を祀る式内社の論社として、三宅島の[[片菅神社]]と競合する分社的性格を有しているため、これらの神社は、元々はそうした三宅島の神社が噴火を避けて移転してきた避難的分社・遥拝所だった可能性がある[http://engishiki.org/izu/bun/iz140116-04.html 片菅神社] - 延喜式神社の調査。 創建は不詳だが、[[康永]]2年（[[1343年]]）再建の記録が『南豆風土誌』に残っている[https://www.town.higashiizu.shizuoka.jp/bg/life/upload/kyo_higashiizutyousi_30mini_6.pdf 『東伊豆町誌』第6章 p.360] - 東伊豆町。また[[貞和]]2年（[[1346年]]）の上梁文などには'''白田来(浜)神社'''（しらだき(はま)じんじゃ）とある（『増訂豆州志稿』）。 また、旧社名や配祀されている祭神・[[五十猛命]]からも分かるように、江戸時代には[[キノミヤ信仰]]を有する来宮神社としての性格も顕著であり、現在も[[河津町]]の[[川津来宮神社]]（[[杉桙別命神社]]）と同じく、「[[河津の鳥精進酒精進|鳥精進・酒精進]]」の文化を有している境内由緒書[https://www.at-s.com/facilities/article/view/temple/137787.html 来宮神社 (東伊豆町)] - 静岡新聞。 == 祭神 == * 志理太乎宜命（しりたおぎのみこと） * [[五十猛命]] == 脚注 == {{Reflist}} == 外部リンク == * [http://www.shizuoka-jinjacho.or.jp/shokai/jinja.php?id=4401004 志理太乎宜神社] - 静岡県神社庁 [[Category:静岡県の神社]] [[Category:東伊豆町の歴史]] [[Category:東伊豆町の建築物]] [[Category:伊豆国の式内社]] ==End Japanese==

|神事 = |地図 = {{Location map|Japan Shizuoka|label=志理太
乎宜神社|coordinates= {{coord2|34|48|8.3|N|139|3|33.8|E|}}|position=left|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} }} '''志理太乎宜神社'''（しりたおぎじんじゃ）は、[[静岡県]][[賀茂郡]][[東伊豆町]]白田（しらだ）にある[[神社]][http://www.shizuoka-jinjacho.or.jp/shokai/jinja.php?id=4401004 志理太乎宜神社] - 静岡県神社庁。江戸時代には'''来宮神社'''（きのみやじんじゃ）と呼ばれており、現在でもこの名が別称として併用され、鳥居の扁額などにもこの名が用いられている[https://kotobank.jp/word/白田村-3062457 白田村とは] - コトバンク[http://engishiki.org/izu/bun/iz140133-02.html 志理太乎宜神社] - 延喜式神社の調査。 == 概要 == [[片瀬白田駅]]の西方至近、[[国道135号]]沿いに鎮座している。 [[三宅島]]の[[椎取神社]]と共に、[[三島大明神]]（[[事代主神]]）の王子の一人・志理太(乎)宜命（しりた(お)き[ぎ]のみこと）を祀る[[式内社]]である'''志理太(乎)宜神社'''（しりた(お)き[ぎ]じんじゃ）の論社となっているが、同時期に創建されたと考えられる北隣りの片瀬（かたせ）地区の[[片菅神社 (東伊豆町)|片菅神社]]（かたすげじんじゃ）もまた、同じく三宅島の三島大明神（事代主神）の別の王子を祀る式内社の論社として、三宅島の[[片菅神社]]と競合する分社的性格を有しているため、これらの神社は、元々はそうした三宅島の神社が噴火を避けて移転してきた避難的分社・遥拝所だった可能性がある[http://engishiki.org/izu/bun/iz140116-04.html 片菅神社] - 延喜式神社の調査。 創建は不詳だが、[[康永]]2年（[[1343年]]）再建の記録が『南豆風土誌』に残っている[https://www.town.higashiizu.shizuoka.jp/bg/life/upload/kyo_higashiizutyousi_30mini_6.pdf 『東伊豆町誌』第6章 p.360] - 東伊豆町。また[[貞和]]2年（[[1346年]]）の上梁文などには'''白田来(浜)神社'''（しらだき(はま)じんじゃ）とある（『増訂豆州志稿』）。 また、旧社名や配祀されている祭神・[[五十猛命]]からも分かるように、江戸時代には[[キノミヤ信仰]]を有する来宮神社としての性格も顕著であり、現在も[[河津町]]の[[川津来宮神社]]（[[杉桙別命神社]]）と同じく、「[[河津の鳥精進酒精進|鳥精進・酒精進]]」の文化を有している境内由緒書[https://www.at-s.com/facilities/article/view/temple/137787.html 来宮神社 (東伊豆町)] - 静岡新聞。 == 祭神 == * 志理太乎宜命（しりたおぎのみこと） * [[五十猛命]] == 脚注 == {{Reflist}} == 外部リンク == * [http://www.shizuoka-jinjacho.or.jp/shokai/jinja.php?id=4401004 志理太乎宜神社] - 静岡県神社庁 ==End Japanese== [[Category:静岡県の神社]] [[Category:東伊豆町の歴史]] [[Category:東伊豆町の建築物]] [[Category:伊豆国の式内社]]

{{nihongo|'''Shiritaogi Shrine'''|志理太乎宜神社}} is a [[shinto shrine]] in the [[Engishiki Jinmyōchō]]. It is located in [[Izu Province]].

said to be the same as (P460) == * {{ill|Shiritakino Shrine|lt=Shiritakino Shrine|WD=Q135039656}} == street address (P6375) == * {'text': '東京都三宅村神着', 'language': 'ja'} [[JA:志理太乎宜神社]] {{wikidata link|Q134892415}} == Japanese content == {{神社 |名称 = 志理太乎宜神社 |画像 = {{画像募集中|cat=賀茂郡}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=14|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=250|frame-height=250|coord={{coord|34|48|8.3|N|139|3|33.8|E}}}} |所在地 = {{jalink|静岡県|Q131320}}{{jalink|賀茂郡|Q961511}}{{jalink|東伊豆町|Q304491}}白田168 |位置 = {{ウィキ座標2段度分秒|34|48|8.3|N|139|3|33.8|E|display=inline,title}} |祭神 = 志理太乎宜命、{{jalink|五十猛命}} |社格 = 旧{{jalink|村社|Q134917284}}、{{jalink|式内社}}論社 |創建 = |本殿 = |別名 = 白田来(浜)神社、(白田)来宮神社 |札所等 = |例祭 = 10月24日-25日 |神事 = |地図 = {{Location map|Japan Shizuoka|label=志理太
乎宜神社|coordinates= {{coord2|34|48|8.3|N|139|3|33.8|E|}}|position=left|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} }} '''志理太乎宜神社'''（しりたおぎじんじゃ）は、{{jalink|静岡県|Q131320}}{{jalink|賀茂郡|Q961511}}{{jalink|東伊豆町|Q304491}}白田（しらだ）にある{{jalink|神社|Q845945}}[http://www.shizuoka-jinjacho.or.jp/shokai/jinja.php?id=4401004 志理太乎宜神社] - 静岡県神社庁。江戸時代には'''来宮神社'''（きのみやじんじゃ）と呼ばれており、現在でもこの名が別称として併用され、鳥居の扁額などにもこの名が用いられている[https://kotobank.jp/word/白田村-3062457 白田村とは] - コトバンク[http://engishiki.org/izu/bun/iz140133-02.html 志理太乎宜神社] - 延喜式神社の調査。 === 概要 === {{jalink|片瀬白田駅|Q6375267}}の西方至近、{{jalink|国道135号|Q6156779}}沿いに鎮座している。 {{jalink|三宅島|Q1075076}}の{{jalink|椎取神社|Q134930749}}と共に、{{jalink|三島大明神}}（{{jalink|事代主神}}）の王子の一人・志理太(乎)宜命（しりた(お)き[ぎ]のみこと）を祀る{{jalink|式内社}}である'''志理太(乎)宜神社'''（しりた(お)き[ぎ]じんじゃ）の論社となっているが、同時期に創建されたと考えられる北隣りの片瀬（かたせ）地区の{{jalink|片菅神社 (東伊豆町)|Q134892414|片菅神社}}（かたすげじんじゃ）もまた、同じく三宅島の三島大明神（事代主神）の別の王子を祀る式内社の論社として、三宅島の{{jalink|片菅神社|Q134930291}}と競合する分社的性格を有しているため、これらの神社は、元々はそうした三宅島の神社が噴火を避けて移転してきた避難的分社・遥拝所だった可能性がある[http://engishiki.org/izu/bun/iz140116-04.html 片菅神社] - 延喜式神社の調査。 創建は不詳だが、{{jalink|康永|Q980266}}2年（{{jalink|1343年|Q6072}}）再建の記録が『南豆風土誌』に残っている[https://www.town.higashiizu.shizuoka.jp/bg/life/upload/kyo_higashiizutyousi_30mini_6.pdf 『東伊豆町誌』第6章 p.360] - 東伊豆町。また{{jalink|貞和|Q1146143}}2年（{{jalink|1346年|Q6080}}）の上梁文などには'''白田来(浜)神社'''（しらだき(はま)じんじゃ）とある（『増訂豆州志稿』）。 また、旧社名や配祀されている祭神・{{jalink|五十猛命}}からも分かるように、江戸時代には{{jalink|キノミヤ信仰|Q11296898}}を有する来宮神社としての性格も顕著であり、現在も{{jalink|河津町|Q1203861}}の{{jalink|川津来宮神社}}（{{jalink|杉桙別命神社|Q11522170}}）と同じく、「{{jalink|河津の鳥精進酒精進|Q11553983|鳥精進・酒精進}}」の文化を有している境内由緒書[https://www.at-s.com/facilities/article/view/temple/137787.html 来宮神社 (東伊豆町)] - 静岡新聞。 === 祭神 === * 志理太乎宜命（しりたおぎのみこと） * {{jalink|五十猛命}} === 脚注 === {{Reflist}} === 外部リンク === * [http://www.shizuoka-jinjacho.or.jp/shokai/jinja.php?id=4401004 志理太乎宜神社] - 静岡県神社庁 {{デフォルトソート:しりたおきしんしや}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages]] [[Category:Autogenerated pages with jawiki interwikis, possibly accidentally overwritten]] [[Category:Shikinai Shōsha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Izu Province]] [[Category:pages]] [[Category:静岡県の神社]] [[Category:東伊豆町の歴史]] [[Category:東伊豆町の建築物]] [[Category:伊豆国の式内社]]

said to be the same as (P460) == * {{ill|Shiritakino Shrine|lt=Shiritakino Shrine|WD=Q135039656}} == street address (P6375) == * {'text': '東京都三宅村神着', 'language': 'ja'} [[JA:志理太乎宜神社]] {{wikidata link|Q134892415}} == Japanese content == {{神社 |名称 = 志理太乎宜神社 |画像 = {{画像募集中|cat=賀茂郡}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=14|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=250|frame-height=250|coord={{coord|34|48|8.3|N|139|3|33.8|E}}}} |所在地 = {{jalink|静岡県|Q131320}}{{jalink|賀茂郡|Q961511}}{{jalink|東伊豆町|Q304491}}白田168 |位置 = {{ウィキ座標2段度分秒|34|48|8.3|N|139|3|33.8|E|display=inline,title}} |祭神 = 志理太乎宜命、{{jalink|五十猛命}} |社格 = 旧{{jalink|村社|Q134917284}}、{{jalink|式内社}}論社 |創建 = |本殿 = |別名 = 白田来(浜)神社、(白田)来宮神社 |札所等 = |例祭 = 10月24日-25日 |神事 = |地図 = {{Location map|Japan Shizuoka|label=志理太
乎宜神社|coordinates= {{coord2|34|48|8.3|N|139|3|33.8|E|}}|position=left|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} }} '''志理太乎宜神社'''（しりたおぎじんじゃ）は、{{jalink|静岡県|Q131320}}{{jalink|賀茂郡|Q961511}}{{jalink|東伊豆町|Q304491}}白田（しらだ）にある{{jalink|神社|Q845945}}[http://www.shizuoka-jinjacho.or.jp/shokai/jinja.php?id=4401004 志理太乎宜神社] - 静岡県神社庁。江戸時代には'''来宮神社'''（きのみやじんじゃ）と呼ばれており、現在でもこの名が別称として併用され、鳥居の扁額などにもこの名が用いられている[https://kotobank.jp/word/白田村-3062457 白田村とは] - コトバンク[http://engishiki.org/izu/bun/iz140133-02.html 志理太乎宜神社] - 延喜式神社の調査。 === 概要 === {{jalink|片瀬白田駅|Q6375267}}の西方至近、{{jalink|国道135号|Q6156779}}沿いに鎮座している。 {{jalink|三宅島|Q1075076}}の{{jalink|椎取神社|Q134930749}}と共に、{{jalink|三島大明神}}（{{jalink|事代主神}}）の王子の一人・志理太(乎)宜命（しりた(お)き[ぎ]のみこと）を祀る{{jalink|式内社}}である'''志理太(乎)宜神社'''（しりた(お)き[ぎ]じんじゃ）の論社となっているが、同時期に創建されたと考えられる北隣りの片瀬（かたせ）地区の{{jalink|片菅神社 (東伊豆町)|Q134892414|片菅神社}}（かたすげじんじゃ）もまた、同じく三宅島の三島大明神（事代主神）の別の王子を祀る式内社の論社として、三宅島の{{jalink|片菅神社|Q134930291}}と競合する分社的性格を有しているため、これらの神社は、元々はそうした三宅島の神社が噴火を避けて移転してきた避難的分社・遥拝所だった可能性がある[http://engishiki.org/izu/bun/iz140116-04.html 片菅神社] - 延喜式神社の調査。 創建は不詳だが、{{jalink|康永|Q980266}}2年（{{jalink|1343年|Q6072}}）再建の記録が『南豆風土誌』に残っている[https://www.town.higashiizu.shizuoka.jp/bg/life/upload/kyo_higashiizutyousi_30mini_6.pdf 『東伊豆町誌』第6章 p.360] - 東伊豆町。また{{jalink|貞和|Q1146143}}2年（{{jalink|1346年|Q6080}}）の上梁文などには'''白田来(浜)神社'''（しらだき(はま)じんじゃ）とある（『増訂豆州志稿』）。 また、旧社名や配祀されている祭神・{{jalink|五十猛命}}からも分かるように、江戸時代には{{jalink|キノミヤ信仰|Q11296898}}を有する来宮神社としての性格も顕著であり、現在も{{jalink|河津町|Q1203861}}の{{jalink|川津来宮神社}}（{{jalink|杉桙別命神社|Q11522170}}）と同じく、「{{jalink|河津の鳥精進酒精進|Q11553983|鳥精進・酒精進}}」の文化を有している境内由緒書[https://www.at-s.com/facilities/article/view/temple/137787.html 来宮神社 (東伊豆町)] - 静岡新聞。 === 祭神 === * 志理太乎宜命（しりたおぎのみこと） * {{jalink|五十猛命}} === 脚注 === {{Reflist}} === 外部リンク === * [http://www.shizuoka-jinjacho.or.jp/shokai/jinja.php?id=4401004 志理太乎宜神社] - 静岡県神社庁 {{デフォルトソート:しりたおきしんしや}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages]] [[Category:Autogenerated pages with jawiki interwikis, possibly accidentally overwritten]] [[Category:Shikinai Shōsha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Izu Province]] [[Category:pages]] [[Category:静岡県の神社]] [[Category:東伊豆町の歴史]] [[Category:東伊豆町の建築物]] [[Category:伊豆国の式内社]] [[Category:need translation]]

said to be the same as (P460) == * {{ill|Shiritakino Shrine|lt=Shiritakino Shrine|WD=Q135039656}} == street address (P6375) == * {'text': '東京都三宅村神着', 'language': 'ja'} [[JA:志理太乎宜神社]] {{wikidata link|Q134892415}} == Japanese content == {{神社 |名称 = 志理太乎宜神社 |画像 = {{画像募集中|cat=賀茂郡}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=14|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=250|frame-height=250|coord={{coord|34|48|8.3|N|139|3|33.8|E}}}} |所在地 = {{jalink|静岡県|Q131320}}{{jalink|賀茂郡|Q961511}}{{jalink|東伊豆町|Q304491}}白田168 |位置 = {{ウィキ座標2段度分秒|34|48|8.3|N|139|3|33.8|E|display=inline,title}} |祭神 = 志理太乎宜命、{{jalink|五十猛命}} |社格 = 旧{{jalink|村社|Q134917284}}、{{jalink|式内社}}論社 |創建 = |本殿 = |別名 = 白田来(浜)神社、(白田)来宮神社 |札所等 = |例祭 = 10月24日-25日 |神事 = |地図 = {{Location map|Japan Shizuoka|label=志理太
乎宜神社|coordinates= {{coord2|34|48|8.3|N|139|3|33.8|E|}}|position=left|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} }} '''志理太乎宜神社'''（しりたおぎじんじゃ）は、{{jalink|静岡県|Q131320}}{{jalink|賀茂郡|Q961511}}{{jalink|東伊豆町|Q304491}}白田（しらだ）にある{{jalink|神社|Q845945}}[http://www.shizuoka-jinjacho.or.jp/shokai/jinja.php?id=4401004 志理太乎宜神社] - 静岡県神社庁。江戸時代には'''来宮神社'''（きのみやじんじゃ）と呼ばれており、現在でもこの名が別称として併用され、鳥居の扁額などにもこの名が用いられている[https://kotobank.jp/word/白田村-3062457 白田村とは] - コトバンク[http://engishiki.org/izu/bun/iz140133-02.html 志理太乎宜神社] - 延喜式神社の調査。 === 概要 === {{jalink|片瀬白田駅|Q6375267}}の西方至近、{{jalink|国道135号|Q6156779}}沿いに鎮座している。 {{jalink|三宅島|Q1075076}}の{{jalink|椎取神社|Q134930749}}と共に、{{jalink|三島大明神}}（{{jalink|事代主神}}）の王子の一人・志理太(乎)宜命（しりた(お)き[ぎ]のみこと）を祀る{{jalink|式内社}}である'''志理太(乎)宜神社'''（しりた(お)き[ぎ]じんじゃ）の論社となっているが、同時期に創建されたと考えられる北隣りの片瀬（かたせ）地区の{{jalink|片菅神社 (東伊豆町)|Q134892414|片菅神社}}（かたすげじんじゃ）もまた、同じく三宅島の三島大明神（事代主神）の別の王子を祀る式内社の論社として、三宅島の{{jalink|片菅神社|Q134930291}}と競合する分社的性格を有しているため、これらの神社は、元々はそうした三宅島の神社が噴火を避けて移転してきた避難的分社・遥拝所だった可能性がある[http://engishiki.org/izu/bun/iz140116-04.html 片菅神社] - 延喜式神社の調査。 創建は不詳だが、{{jalink|康永|Q980266}}2年（{{jalink|1343年|Q6072}}）再建の記録が『南豆風土誌』に残っている[https://www.town.higashiizu.shizuoka.jp/bg/life/upload/kyo_higashiizutyousi_30mini_6.pdf 『東伊豆町誌』第6章 p.360] - 東伊豆町。また{{jalink|貞和|Q1146143}}2年（{{jalink|1346年|Q6080}}）の上梁文などには'''白田来(浜)神社'''（しらだき(はま)じんじゃ）とある（『増訂豆州志稿』）。 また、旧社名や配祀されている祭神・{{jalink|五十猛命}}からも分かるように、江戸時代には{{jalink|キノミヤ信仰|Q11296898}}を有する来宮神社としての性格も顕著であり、現在も{{jalink|河津町|Q1203861}}の{{jalink|川津来宮神社}}（{{jalink|杉桙別命神社|Q11522170}}）と同じく、「{{jalink|河津の鳥精進酒精進|Q11553983|鳥精進・酒精進}}」の文化を有している境内由緒書[https://www.at-s.com/facilities/article/view/temple/137787.html 来宮神社 (東伊豆町)] - 静岡新聞。 === 祭神 === * 志理太乎宜命（しりたおぎのみこと） * {{jalink|五十猛命}} === 脚注 === {{Reflist}} === 外部リンク === * [http://www.shizuoka-jinjacho.or.jp/shokai/jinja.php?id=4401004 志理太乎宜神社] - 静岡県神社庁 {{デフォルトソート:しりたおきしんしや}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages]] [[Category:Shikinai Shōsha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Izu Province]] [[Category:pages]] [[Category:静岡県の神社]] [[Category:東伊豆町の歴史]] [[Category:東伊豆町の建築物]] [[Category:伊豆国の式内社]] [[Category:need translation]]

link|Q134892415}} == Japanese content == {{Shrine |Name = Shiritaogi Shrine |Image = {{Image wanted|cat=Kamo District}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=14|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=250|frame-height=250|coord={{coord|34|48|8.3|N|139|3|33.8|E}}}} |Location = {{jalink|Shizuoka Prefecture|Q131320}}{{jalink|Kamo District|Q961511}}{{jalink|Higashiizu Town|Q304491}} Shirada 168 |Coordinates = {{Wiki coordinates DMS|34|48|8.3|N|139|3|33.8|E|display=inline,title}} |Enshrined deities = Shiritaogi-no-Mikoto, {{jalink|Isotakeru-no-Mikoto}} |Shrine rank = Former {{jalink|village shrine|Q134917284}}, candidate {{jalink|Shikinaisha}} |Founded = |Main hall = |Other names = Shirada Ki(hama) Shrine, (Shirada) Kinomiya Shrine |Pilgrimage status etc. = |Annual festival = October 24–25 |Rituals = |Map = {{Location map|Japan Shizuoka|label=Shirita
ogi Shrine|coordinates= {{coord2|34|48|8.3|N|139|3|33.8|E|}}|position=left|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} }} '''Shiritaogi Shrine''' (しりたおぎじんじゃ) is a {{jalink|Shinto shrine|Q845945}} located in Shirada, {{jalink|Higashiizu Town|Q304491}}, {{jalink|Kamo District|Q961511}}, {{jalink|Shizuoka Prefecture|Q131320}}[http://www.shizuoka-jinjacho.or.jp/shokai/jinja.php?id=4401004 Shiritaogi Shrine] - Shizuoka Prefecture Association of Shinto Shrines. During the Edo period it was called '''Kinomiya Shrine''' (きのみやじんじゃ), and this name is still used today as an alternative name, appearing on items such as the plaque on the torii gate[https://kotobank.jp/word/白田村-3062457 What is Shirada Village] - Kotobank[http://engishiki.org/izu/bun/iz140133-02.html Shiritaogi Shrine] - Survey of Engishiki Shrines. === Overview === The shrine is located immediately west of {{jalink|Katase-Shirata Station|Q6375267}}, along {{jalink|National Route 135|Q6156779}}. Together with {{jalink|Shiitore Shrine|Q134930749}} on {{jalink|Miyakejima|Q1075076}}, it is regarded as a candidate shrine (ronsha) of '''Shirita(ogi) Shrine''' (しりた(お)き[ぎ]じんじゃ), a {{jalink|Shikinaisha}} that enshrines Shirita(ogi)-no-Mikoto, one of the princes of {{jalink|Mishima Daimyōjin}} ({{jalink|Kotoshironushi-no-Kami}}). Meanwhile, {{jalink|Katasuge Shrine (Higashiizu Town)|Q134892414|Katasuge Shrine}} in the neighboring Katase district to the north—thought to have been founded around the same time—also serves as a candidate Shikinaisha enshrining another prince of Mishima Daimyōjin (Kotoshironushi-no-Kami) of Miyakejima, competing with {{jalink|Katasuge Shrine|Q134930291}} on Miyakejima itself. Because of this branch-shrine–like relationship, it is possible that these shrines were originally established as refugee branch shrines or distant worship sites relocated from Miyakejima to avoid volcanic eruptions[http://engishiki.org/izu/bun/iz140116-04.html Katasuge Shrine] - Survey of Engishiki Shrines. Although the date of foundation is unknown, a record of reconstruction in the 2nd year of {{jalink|Kōei|Q980266}} ({{jalink|1343|Q6072}}) appears in the *Nantō Fudoki*[https://www.town.higashiizu.shizuoka.jp/bg/life/upload/kyo_higashiizutyousi_30mini_6.pdf *History of Higashiizu Town*, Chapter 6, p. 360] - Higashiizu Town. In addition, ridgepole inscriptions from the 2nd year of {{jalink|Jōwa|Q1146143}} ({{jalink|1346|Q6080}}) record the name '''Shirada Ki(hama) Shrine''' (しらだき(はま)じんじゃ) (*Zōtei Tōshū Shikō*). As indicated by its former shrine name and the enshrined deity {{jalink|Isotakeru-no-Mikoto}}, during the Edo period it also strongly exhibited the character of a Kinomiya Shrine associated with {{jalink|Kinomiya faith|Q11296898}}. Even today, like {{jalink|Kawazu Kinomiya Shrine}} ({{jalink|Sugikubetsu-no-Mikoto Shrine|Q11522170}}) in {{jalink|Kawazu Town|Q1203861}}, it retains the cultural tradition known as “{{jalink|Bird abstinence and sake abstinence of Kawazu|Q11553983|bird abstinence and sake abstinence}}”On-site shrine history plaque[https://www.at-s.com/facilities/article/view/temple/137787.html Kinomiya Shrine (Higashiizu Town)] - Shizuoka Shimbun. === Enshrined deities === * Shiritaogi-no-Mikoto * {{jalink|Isotakeru-no-Mikoto}} === Notes === {{Reflist}} === External links === * [http://www.shizuoka-jinjacho.or.jp/shokai/jinja.php?id=4401004 Shiritaogi Shrine] - Shizuoka Prefecture Association of Shinto Shrines {{DEFAULTSORT:Shiritaogijinja}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages]] [[Category:Shikinai Shōsha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Izu Province]] [[Category:pages]] [[Category:静岡県の神社]] [[Category:東伊豆町の歴史]] [[Category:東伊豆町の建築物]] [[Category:伊豆国の式内社]] [[Category:need translation]]

said to be the same as (P460) == * {{ill|Shiritakino Shrine|lt=Shiritakino Shrine|WD=Q135039656}} == street address (P6375) == * {'text': '東京都三宅村神着', 'language': 'ja'} [[JA:志理太乎宜神社]] {{wikidata link|Q134892415}} == Japanese content == {{Shrine |Name = Shiritaogi Shrine |Image = {{Image wanted|cat=Kamo District}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=14|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=250|frame-height=250|coord={{coord|34|48|8.3|N|139|3|33.8|E}}}} |Location = {{jalink|Shizuoka Prefecture|Q131320}}{{jalink|Kamo District|Q961511}}{{jalink|Higashiizu Town|Q304491}} Shirada 168 |Coordinates = {{Wiki coordinates DMS|34|48|8.3|N|139|3|33.8|E|display=inline,title}} |Enshrined deities = Shiritaogi-no-Mikoto, {{jalink|Isotakeru-no-Mikoto}} |Shrine rank = Former {{jalink|village shrine|Q134917284}}, candidate {{jalink|Shikinaisha}} |Founded = |Main hall = |Other names = Shirada Ki(hama) Shrine, (Shirada) Kinomiya Shrine |Pilgrimage status etc. = |Annual festival = October 24–25 |Rituals = |Map = {{Location map|Japan Shizuoka|label=Shirita
ogi Shrine|coordinates= {{coord2|34|48|8.3|N|139|3|33.8|E|}}|position=left|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} }} '''Shiritaogi Shrine''' (しりたおぎじんじゃ) is a {{jalink|Shinto shrine|Q845945}} located in Shirada, {{jalink|Higashiizu Town|Q304491}}, {{jalink|Kamo District|Q961511}}, {{jalink|Shizuoka Prefecture|Q131320}}[http://www.shizuoka-jinjacho.or.jp/shokai/jinja.php?id=4401004 Shiritaogi Shrine] - Shizuoka Prefecture Association of Shinto Shrines. During the Edo period it was called '''Kinomiya Shrine''' (きのみやじんじゃ), and this name is still used today as an alternative name, appearing on items such as the plaque on the torii gate[https://kotobank.jp/word/白田村-3062457 What is Shirada Village] - Kotobank[http://engishiki.org/izu/bun/iz140133-02.html Shiritaogi Shrine] - Survey of Engishiki Shrines. === Overview === The shrine is located immediately west of {{jalink|Katase-Shirata Station|Q6375267}}, along {{jalink|National Route 135|Q6156779}}. Together with {{jalink|Shiitore Shrine|Q134930749}} on {{jalink|Miyakejima|Q1075076}}, it is regarded as a candidate shrine (ronsha) of '''Shirita(ogi) Shrine''' (しりた(お)き[ぎ]じんじゃ), a {{jalink|Shikinaisha}} that enshrines Shirita(ogi)-no-Mikoto, one of the princes of {{jalink|Mishima Daimyōjin}} ({{jalink|Kotoshironushi-no-Kami}}). Meanwhile, {{jalink|Katasuge Shrine (Higashiizu Town)|Q134892414|Katasuge Shrine}} in the neighboring Katase district to the north—thought to have been founded around the same time—also serves as a candidate Shikinaisha enshrining another prince of Mishima Daimyōjin (Kotoshironushi-no-Kami) of Miyakejima, competing with {{jalink|Katasuge Shrine|Q134930291}} on Miyakejima itself. Because of this branch-shrine–like relationship, it is possible that these shrines were originally established as refugee branch shrines or distant worship sites relocated from Miyakejima to avoid volcanic eruptions[http://engishiki.org/izu/bun/iz140116-04.html Katasuge Shrine] - Survey of Engishiki Shrines. Although the date of foundation is unknown, a record of reconstruction in the 2nd year of {{jalink|Kōei|Q980266}} ({{jalink|1343|Q6072}}) appears in the *Nantō Fudoki*[https://www.town.higashiizu.shizuoka.jp/bg/life/upload/kyo_higashiizutyousi_30mini_6.pdf *History of Higashiizu Town*, Chapter 6, p. 360] - Higashiizu Town. In addition, ridgepole inscriptions from the 2nd year of {{jalink|Jōwa|Q1146143}} ({{jalink|1346|Q6080}}) record the name '''Shirada Ki(hama) Shrine''' (しらだき(はま)じんじゃ) (*Zōtei Tōshū Shikō*). As indicated by its former shrine name and the enshrined deity {{jalink|Isotakeru-no-Mikoto}}, during the Edo period it also strongly exhibited the character of a Kinomiya Shrine associated with {{jalink|Kinomiya faith|Q11296898}}. Even today, like {{jalink|Kawazu Kinomiya Shrine}} ({{jalink|Sugikubetsu-no-Mikoto Shrine|Q11522170}}) in {{jalink|Kawazu Town|Q1203861}}, it retains the cultural tradition known as “{{jalink|Bird abstinence and sake abstinence of Kawazu|Q11553983|bird abstinence and sake abstinence}}”On-site shrine history plaque[https://www.at-s.com/facilities/article/view/temple/137787.html Kinomiya Shrine (Higashiizu Town)] - Shizuoka Shimbun. === Enshrined deities === * Shiritaogi-no-Mikoto * {{jalink|Isotakeru-no-Mikoto}} === Notes === {{Reflist}} === External links === * [http://www.shizuoka-jinjacho.or.jp/shokai/jinja.php?id=4401004 Shiritaogi Shrine] - Shizuoka Prefecture Association of Shinto Shrines {{DEFAULTSORT:Shiritaogijinja}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages]] [[Category:Shikinai Shōsha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Izu Province]] [[Category:pages]] [[Category:静岡県の神社]] [[Category:東伊豆町の歴史]] [[Category:東伊豆町の建築物]] [[Category:伊豆国の式内社]]

link|Q134892415}} == Japanese content == {{Shrine |Name = Shiritaogi Shrine |Image = {{Image wanted|cat=Kamo District}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=14|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=250|frame-height=250|coord={{coord|34|48|8.3|N|139|3|33.8|E}}}} |Location = {{jalink|Shizuoka Prefecture|Q131320}}{{jalink|Kamo District|Q961511}}{{jalink|Higashiizu Town|Q304491}} Shirada 168 |Coordinates = {{Wiki coordinates DMS|34|48|8.3|N|139|3|33.8|E|display=inline,title}} |Enshrined deities = Shiritaogi-no-Mikoto, {{jalink|Isotakeru-no-Mikoto}} |Shrine rank = Former {{jalink|village shrine|Q134917284}}, candidate {{jalink|Shikinaisha}} |Founded = |Main hall = |Other names = Shirada Ki(hama) Shrine, (Shirada) Kinomiya Shrine |Pilgrimage status etc. = |Annual festival = October 24–25 |Rituals = |Map = {{Location map|Japan Shizuoka|label=Shirita
ogi Shrine|coordinates= {{coord2|34|48|8.3|N|139|3|33.8|E|}}|position=left|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} }} '''Shiritaogi Shrine''' (しりたおぎじんじゃ) is a {{jalink|Shinto shrine|Q845945}} located in Shirada, {{jalink|Higashiizu Town|Q304491}}, {{jalink|Kamo District|Q961511}}, {{jalink|Shizuoka Prefecture|Q131320}}[http://www.shizuoka-jinjacho.or.jp/shokai/jinja.php?id=4401004 Shiritaogi Shrine] - Shizuoka Prefecture Association of Shinto Shrines. During the Edo period it was called '''Kinomiya Shrine''' (きのみやじんじゃ), and this name is still used today as an alternative name, appearing on items such as the plaque on the torii gate[https://kotobank.jp/word/白田村-3062457 What is Shirada Village] - Kotobank[http://engishiki.org/izu/bun/iz140133-02.html Shiritaogi Shrine] - Survey of Engishiki Shrines. === Overview === The shrine is located immediately west of {{jalink|Katase-Shirata Station|Q6375267}}, along {{jalink|National Route 135|Q6156779}}. Together with {{jalink|Shiitore Shrine|Q134930749}} on {{jalink|Miyakejima|Q1075076}}, it is regarded as a candidate shrine (ronsha) of '''Shirita(ogi) Shrine''' (しりた(お)き[ぎ]じんじゃ), a {{jalink|Shikinaisha}} that enshrines Shirita(ogi)-no-Mikoto, one of the princes of {{jalink|Mishima Daimyōjin}} ({{jalink|Kotoshironushi-no-Kami}}). Meanwhile, {{jalink|Katasuge Shrine (Higashiizu Town)|Q134892414|Katasuge Shrine}} in the neighboring Katase district to the north—thought to have been founded around the same time—also serves as a candidate Shikinaisha enshrining another prince of Mishima Daimyōjin (Kotoshironushi-no-Kami) of Miyakejima, competing with {{jalink|Katasuge Shrine|Q134930291}} on Miyakejima itself. Because of this branch-shrine–like relationship, it is possible that these shrines were originally established as refugee branch shrines or distant worship sites relocated from Miyakejima to avoid volcanic eruptions[http://engishiki.org/izu/bun/iz140116-04.html Katasuge Shrine] - Survey of Engishiki Shrines. Although the date of foundation is unknown, a record of reconstruction in the 2nd year of {{jalink|Kōei|Q980266}} ({{jalink|1343|Q6072}}) appears in the *Nantō Fudoki*[https://www.town.higashiizu.shizuoka.jp/bg/life/upload/kyo_higashiizutyousi_30mini_6.pdf *History of Higashiizu Town*, Chapter 6, p. 360] - Higashiizu Town. In addition, ridgepole inscriptions from the 2nd year of {{jalink|Jōwa|Q1146143}} ({{jalink|1346|Q6080}}) record the name '''Shirada Ki(hama) Shrine''' (しらだき(はま)じんじゃ) (*Zōtei Tōshū Shikō*). As indicated by its former shrine name and the enshrined deity {{jalink|Isotakeru-no-Mikoto}}, during the Edo period it also strongly exhibited the character of a Kinomiya Shrine associated with {{jalink|Kinomiya faith|Q11296898}}. Even today, like {{jalink|Kawazu Kinomiya Shrine}} ({{jalink|Sugikubetsu-no-Mikoto Shrine|Q11522170}}) in {{jalink|Kawazu Town|Q1203861}}, it retains the cultural tradition known as “{{jalink|Bird abstinence and sake abstinence of Kawazu|Q11553983|bird abstinence and sake abstinence}}”On-site shrine history plaque[https://www.at-s.com/facilities/article/view/temple/137787.html Kinomiya Shrine (Higashiizu Town)] - Shizuoka Shimbun. === Enshrined deities === * Shiritaogi-no-Mikoto * {{jalink|Isotakeru-no-Mikoto}} === Notes === {{Reflist}} === External links === * [http://www.shizuoka-jinjacho.or.jp/shokai/jinja.php?id=4401004 Shiritaogi Shrine] - Shizuoka Prefecture Association of Shinto Shrines {{DEFAULTSORT:Shiritaogijinja}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Shiritaogi Shrine]][[Category:Shikinaisha]] [[Category:Shikinai Shōsha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Izu Province]] [[Category:pages]] [[Category:静岡県の神社]] [[Category:東伊豆町の歴史]] [[Category:東伊豆町の建築物]] [[Category:伊豆国の式内社]]

link|Q134892415}} == Japanese content == {{Shrine |Name = Shiritaogi Shrine |Image = {{Image wanted|cat=Kamo District}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=14|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=250|frame-height=250|coord={{coord|34|48|8.3|N|139|3|33.8|E}}}} |Location = {{jalink|Shizuoka Prefecture|Q131320}}{{jalink|Kamo District|Q961511}}{{jalink|Higashiizu Town|Q304491}} Shirada 168 |Coordinates = {{Wiki coordinates DMS|34|48|8.3|N|139|3|33.8|E|display=inline,title}} |Enshrined deities = Shiritaogi-no-Mikoto, {{jalink|Isotakeru-no-Mikoto}} |Shrine rank = Former {{jalink|village shrine|Q134917284}}, candidate {{jalink|Shikinaisha}} |Founded = |Main hall = |Other names = Shirada Ki(hama) Shrine, (Shirada) Kinomiya Shrine |Pilgrimage status etc. = |Annual festival = October 24–25 |Rituals = |Map = {{Location map|Japan Shizuoka|label=Shirita
ogi Shrine|coordinates= {{coord2|34|48|8.3|N|139|3|33.8|E|}}|position=left|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} }} '''Shiritaogi Shrine''' (しりたおぎじんじゃ) is a {{jalink|Shinto shrine|Q845945}} located in Shirada, {{jalink|Higashiizu Town|Q304491}}, {{jalink|Kamo District|Q961511}}, {{jalink|Shizuoka Prefecture|Q131320}}[http://www.shizuoka-jinjacho.or.jp/shokai/jinja.php?id=4401004 Shiritaogi Shrine] - Shizuoka Prefecture Association of Shinto Shrines. During the Edo period it was called '''Kinomiya Shrine''' (きのみやじんじゃ), and this name is still used today as an alternative name, appearing on items such as the plaque on the torii gate[https://kotobank.jp/word/白田村-3062457 What is Shirada Village] - Kotobank[http://engishiki.org/izu/bun/iz140133-02.html Shiritaogi Shrine] - Survey of Engishiki Shrines. === Overview === The shrine is located immediately west of {{jalink|Katase-Shirata Station|Q6375267}}, along {{jalink|National Route 135|Q6156779}}. Together with {{jalink|Shiitore Shrine|Q134930749}} on {{jalink|Miyakejima|Q1075076}}, it is regarded as a candidate shrine (ronsha) of '''Shirita(ogi) Shrine''' (しりた(お)き[ぎ]じんじゃ), a {{jalink|Shikinaisha}} that enshrines Shirita(ogi)-no-Mikoto, one of the princes of {{jalink|Mishima Daimyōjin}} ({{jalink|Kotoshironushi-no-Kami}}). Meanwhile, {{jalink|Katasuge Shrine (Higashiizu Town)|Q134892414|Katasuge Shrine}} in the neighboring Katase district to the north—thought to have been founded around the same time—also serves as a candidate Shikinaisha enshrining another prince of Mishima Daimyōjin (Kotoshironushi-no-Kami) of Miyakejima, competing with {{jalink|Katasuge Shrine|Q134930291}} on Miyakejima itself. Because of this branch-shrine–like relationship, it is possible that these shrines were originally established as refugee branch shrines or distant worship sites relocated from Miyakejima to avoid volcanic eruptions[http://engishiki.org/izu/bun/iz140116-04.html Katasuge Shrine] - Survey of Engishiki Shrines. Although the date of foundation is unknown, a record of reconstruction in the 2nd year of {{jalink|Kōei|Q980266}} ({{jalink|1343|Q6072}}) appears in the *Nantō Fudoki*[https://www.town.higashiizu.shizuoka.jp/bg/life/upload/kyo_higashiizutyousi_30mini_6.pdf *History of Higashiizu Town*, Chapter 6, p. 360] - Higashiizu Town. In addition, ridgepole inscriptions from the 2nd year of {{jalink|Jōwa|Q1146143}} ({{jalink|1346|Q6080}}) record the name '''Shirada Ki(hama) Shrine''' (しらだき(はま)じんじゃ) (*Zōtei Tōshū Shikō*). As indicated by its former shrine name and the enshrined deity {{jalink|Isotakeru-no-Mikoto}}, during the Edo period it also strongly exhibited the character of a Kinomiya Shrine associated with {{jalink|Kinomiya faith|Q11296898}}. Even today, like {{jalink|Kawazu Kinomiya Shrine}} ({{jalink|Sugikubetsu-no-Mikoto Shrine|Q11522170}}) in {{jalink|Kawazu Town|Q1203861}}, it retains the cultural tradition known as “{{jalink|Bird abstinence and sake abstinence of Kawazu|Q11553983|bird abstinence and sake abstinence}}”On-site shrine history plaque[https://www.at-s.com/facilities/article/view/temple/137787.html Kinomiya Shrine (Higashiizu Town)] - Shizuoka Shimbun. === Enshrined deities === * Shiritaogi-no-Mikoto * {{jalink|Isotakeru-no-Mikoto}} === Notes === {{Reflist}} === External links === * [http://www.shizuoka-jinjacho.or.jp/shokai/jinja.php?id=4401004 Shiritaogi Shrine] - Shizuoka Prefecture Association of Shinto Shrines {{DEFAULTSORT:Shiritaogijinja}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Shiritaogi Shrine]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Shiritaogi Shrine]][[Category:Pages]] [[Category:Shikinai Shōsha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Izu Province]] [[Category:pages]] [[Category:静岡県の神社]] [[Category:東伊豆町の歴史]] [[Category:東伊豆町の建築物]] [[Category:伊豆国の式内社]]

Together with {{jalink|Shiitore Shrine|Q134930749}} on {{jalink|Miyakejima|Q1075076}}, it is regarded as a candidate shrine (ronsha) of '''Shirita(ogi) Shrine''' (しりた(お)き[ぎ]じんじゃ), a {{jalink|Shikinaisha}} that enshrines Shirita(ogi)-no-Mikoto, one of the princes of {{jalink|Mishima Daimyōjin}} ({{jalink|Kotoshironushi-no-Kami}}). Although the date of foundation is unknown, a record of reconstruction in the 2nd year of {{jalink|Kōei|Q980266}} ({{jalink|1343|Q6072}}) appears in the *Nantō Fudoki*[https://www.town.higashiizu.shizuoka.jp/bg/life/upload/kyo_higashiizutyousi_30mini_6.pdf *History of Higashiizu Town*, Chapter 6, p. 360] - Higashiizu Town. In addition, ridgepole inscriptions from the 2nd year of {{jalink|Jōwa|Q1146143}} ({{jalink|1346|Q6080}}) record the name '''Shirada Ki(hama) Shrine''' (しらだき(はま)じんじゃ) (*Zōtei Tōshū Shikō*). As indicated by its former shrine name and the enshrined deity {{jalink|Isotakeru-no-Mikoto}}, during the Edo period it also strongly exhibited the character of a Kinomiya Shrine associated with {{jalink|Kinomiya faith|Q11296898}}. Even today, like {{jalink|Kawazu Kinomiya Shrine}} ({{jalink|Sugikubetsu-no-Mikoto Shrine|Q11522170}}) in {{jalink|Kawazu Town|Q1203861}}, it retains the cultural tradition known as “{{jalink|Bird abstinence and sake abstinence of Kawazu|Q11553983|bird abstinence and sake abstinence}}”On-site shrine history plaque[https://www.at-s.com/facilities/article/view/temple/137787.html Kinomiya Shrine (Higashiizu Town)] - Shizuoka Shimbun. === Enshrined deities === * Shiritaogi-no-Mikoto * {{jalink|Isotakeru-no-Mikoto}} === Notes === {{Reflist}} === External links === * [http://www.shizuoka-jinjacho.or.jp/shokai/jinja.php?id=4401004 Shiritaogi Shrine] - Shizuoka Prefecture Association of Shinto Shrines {{DEFAULTSORT:Shiritaogijinja}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Shiritaogi Shrine]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Shiritaogi Shrine]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shikinai Shōsha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Izu Province]] [[Category:pages]] [[Category:静岡県の神社]] [[Category:東伊豆町の歴史]] [[Category:東伊豆町の建築物]] [[Category:伊豆国の式内社]]

Prefecture|Q131320|WD=Q131320}}{{ill|lt=Kamo District|Q961511|WD=Q961511}}{{ill|lt=Higashiizu Town|Q304491|WD=Q304491}} Shirada 168 |Coordinates = {{Wiki coordinates DMS|34|48|8.3|N|139|3|33.8|E|display=inline,title}} |Enshrined deities = Shiritaogi-no-Mikoto, {{ill||lt=Isotakeru-no-Mikoto|UNKNOWN}} |Shrine rank = Former {{ill|lt=village shrine|Q134917284|WD=Q134917284}}, candidate {{ill||lt=Shikinaisha|UNKNOWN}} |Founded = |Main hall = |Other names = Shirada Ki(hama) Shrine, (Shirada) Kinomiya Shrine |Pilgrimage status etc. = |Annual festival = October 24–25 |Rituals = |Map = {{Location map|Japan Shizuoka|label=Shirita
ogi Shrine|coordinates= {{coord2|34|48|8.3|N|139|3|33.8|E|}}|position=left|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} }} '''Shiritaogi Shrine''' (しりたおぎじんじゃ) is a {{ill|lt=Shinto shrine|Q845945|WD=Q845945}} located in Shirada, {{ill|lt=Higashiizu Town|Q304491|WD=Q304491}}, {{ill|lt=Kamo District|Q961511|WD=Q961511}}, {{ill|lt=Shizuoka Prefecture|Q131320|WD=Q131320}}[http://www.shizuoka-jinjacho.or.jp/shokai/jinja.php?id=4401004 Shiritaogi Shrine] - Shizuoka Prefecture Association of Shinto Shrines. During the Edo period it was called '''Kinomiya Shrine''' (きのみやじんじゃ), and this name is still used today as an alternative name, appearing on items such as the plaque on the torii gate[https://kotobank.jp/word/白田村-3062457 What is Shirada Village] - Kotobank[http://engishiki.org/izu/bun/iz140133-02.html Shiritaogi Shrine] - Survey of Engishiki Shrines. === Overview === The shrine is located immediately west of {{ill|lt=Katase-Shirata Station|Q6375267|WD=Q6375267}}, along {{ill|lt=National Route 135|Q6156779|WD=Q6156779}}. Together with {{ill|lt=Shiitore Shrine|Q134930749|WD=Q134930749}} on {{ill|lt=Miyakejima|Q1075076|WD=Q1075076}}, it is regarded as a candidate shrine (ronsha) of '''Shirita(ogi) Shrine''' (しりた(お)き[ぎ]じんじゃ), a {{ill||lt=Shikinaisha|UNKNOWN}} that enshrines Shirita(ogi)-no-Mikoto, one of the princes of {{ill||lt=Mishima Daimyōjin|UNKNOWN}} ({{ill||lt=Kotoshironushi-no-Kami|UNKNOWN}}). Meanwhile, {{ill|lt=Katasuge Shrine (Higashiizu Town)|Q134892414|WD=Q134892414|lt=Katasuge Shrine}} in the neighboring Katase district to the north—thought to have been founded around the same time—also serves as a candidate Shikinaisha enshrining another prince of Mishima Daimyōjin (Kotoshironushi-no-Kami) of Miyakejima, competing with {{ill|lt=Katasuge Shrine|Q134930291|WD=Q134930291}} on Miyakejima itself. Because of this branch-shrine–like relationship, it is possible that these shrines were originally established as refugee branch shrines or distant worship sites relocated from Miyakejima to avoid volcanic eruptions[http://engishiki.org/izu/bun/iz140116-04.html Katasuge Shrine] - Survey of Engishiki Shrines. Although the date of foundation is unknown, a record of reconstruction in the 2nd year of {{ill|lt=Kōei|Q980266|WD=Q980266}} ({{ill|lt=1343|Q6072|WD=Q6072}}) appears in the *Nantō Fudoki*[https://www.town.higashiizu.shizuoka.jp/bg/life/upload/kyo_higashiizutyousi_30mini_6.pdf *History of Higashiizu Town*, Chapter 6, p. 360] - Higashiizu Town. In addition, ridgepole inscriptions from the 2nd year of {{ill|lt=Jōwa|Q1146143|WD=Q1146143}} ({{ill|lt=1346|Q6080|WD=Q6080}}) record the name '''Shirada Ki(hama) Shrine''' (しらだき(はま)じんじゃ) (*Zōtei Tōshū Shikō*). As indicated by its former shrine name and the enshrined deity {{ill||lt=Isotakeru-no-Mikoto|UNKNOWN}}, during the Edo period it also strongly exhibited the character of a Kinomiya Shrine associated with {{ill|lt=Kinomiya faith|Q11296898|WD=Q11296898}}. Even today, like {{ill||lt=Kawazu Kinomiya Shrine|UNKNOWN}} ({{ill|lt=Sugikubetsu-no-Mikoto Shrine|Q11522170|WD=Q11522170}}) in {{ill|lt=Kawazu Town|Q1203861|WD=Q1203861}}, it retains the cultural tradition known as “{{ill|lt=Bird abstinence and sake abstinence of Kawazu|Q11553983|WD=Q11553983|lt=bird abstinence and sake abstinence}}”On-site shrine history plaque[https://www.at-s.com/facilities/article/view/temple/137787.html Kinomiya Shrine (Higashiizu Town)] - Shizuoka Shimbun. === Enshrined deities === * Shiritaogi-no-Mikoto * {{ill||lt=Isotakeru-no-Mikoto|UNKNOWN}} === Notes === {{Reflist}} === External links === * [http://www.shizuoka-jinjacho.or.jp/shokai/jinja.php?id=4401004 Shiritaogi Shrine] - Shizuoka Prefecture Association of Shinto Shrines {{DEFAULTSORT:Shiritaogijinja}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Shiritaogi Shrine]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Shiritaogi Shrine]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shikinai Shōsha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Izu Province]] [[Category:pages]] [[Category:静岡県の神社]] [[Category:東伊豆町の歴史]] [[Category:東伊豆町の建築物]] [[Category:伊豆国の式内社]]

said to be the same as (P460) == * {{ill|Shiritakino Shrine|lt=Shiritakino Shrine|WD=Q135039656}} == street address (P6375) == * {'text': '東京都三宅村神着', 'language': 'ja'} [[JA:志理太乎宜神社]] {{wikidata link|Q134892415}} == Japanese content == {{Shrine |Name = Shiritaogi Shrine |Image = {{Image wanted|cat=Kamo District}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=14|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=250|frame-height=250|coord={{coord|34|48|8.3|N|139|3|33.8|E}}}} |Location = {{ill|lt=Shizuoka Prefecture|Q131320|WD=Q131320}}{{ill|lt=Kamo District|Q961511|WD=Q961511}}{{ill|lt=Higashiizu Town|Q304491|WD=Q304491}} Shirada 168 |Coordinates = {{Wiki coordinates DMS|34|48|8.3|N|139|3|33.8|E|display=inline,title}} |Enshrined deities = Shiritaogi-no-Mikoto, {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Isotakeru-no-Mikoto|UNKNOWN}} |Shrine rank = Former {{ill|lt=village shrine|Q134917284|WD=Q134917284}}, candidate {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|UNKNOWN}} |Founded = |Main hall = |Other names = Shirada Ki(hama) Shrine, (Shirada) Kinomiya Shrine |Pilgrimage status etc. = |Annual festival = October 24–25 |Rituals = |Map = {{Location map|Japan Shizuoka|label=Shirita
ogi Shrine|coordinates= {{coord2|34|48|8.3|N|139|3|33.8|E|}}|position=left|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} }} '''Shiritaogi Shrine''' (しりたおぎじんじゃ) is a {{ill|lt=Shinto shrine|Q845945|WD=Q845945}} located in Shirada, {{ill|lt=Higashiizu Town|Q304491|WD=Q304491}}, {{ill|lt=Kamo District|Q961511|WD=Q961511}}, {{ill|lt=Shizuoka Prefecture|Q131320|WD=Q131320}}[http://www.shizuoka-jinjacho.or.jp/shokai/jinja.php?id=4401004 Shiritaogi Shrine] - Shizuoka Prefecture Association of Shinto Shrines. During the Edo period it was called '''Kinomiya Shrine''' (きのみやじんじゃ), and this name is still used today as an alternative name, appearing on items such as the plaque on the torii gate[https://kotobank.jp/word/白田村-3062457 What is Shirada Village] - Kotobank[http://engishiki.org/izu/bun/iz140133-02.html Shiritaogi Shrine] - Survey of Engishiki Shrines. === Overview === The shrine is located immediately west of {{ill|lt=Katase-Shirata Station|Q6375267|WD=Q6375267}}, along {{ill|lt=National Route 135|Q6156779|WD=Q6156779}}. Together with {{ill|lt=Shiitore Shrine|Q134930749|WD=Q134930749}} on {{ill|lt=Miyakejima|Q1075076|WD=Q1075076}}, it is regarded as a candidate shrine (ronsha) of '''Shirita(ogi) Shrine''' (しりた(お)き[ぎ]じんじゃ), a {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|UNKNOWN}} that enshrines Shirita(ogi)-no-Mikoto, one of the princes of {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Mishima Daimyōjin|UNKNOWN}} ({{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Kotoshironushi-no-Kami|UNKNOWN}}). Meanwhile, {{ill|lt=Katasuge Shrine (Higashiizu Town)|Q134892414|WD=Q134892414|lt=Katasuge Shrine}} in the neighboring Katase district to the north—thought to have been founded around the same time—also serves as a candidate Shikinaisha enshrining another prince of Mishima Daimyōjin (Kotoshironushi-no-Kami) of Miyakejima, competing with {{ill|lt=Katasuge Shrine|Q134930291|WD=Q134930291}} on Miyakejima itself. Because of this branch-shrine–like relationship, it is possible that these shrines were originally established as refugee branch shrines or distant worship sites relocated from Miyakejima to avoid volcanic eruptions[http://engishiki.org/izu/bun/iz140116-04.html Katasuge Shrine] - Survey of Engishiki Shrines. Although the date of foundation is unknown, a record of reconstruction in the 2nd year of {{ill|lt=Kōei|Q980266|WD=Q980266}} ({{ill|lt=1343|Q6072|WD=Q6072}}) appears in the *Nantō Fudoki*[https://www.town.higashiizu.shizuoka.jp/bg/life/upload/kyo_higashiizutyousi_30mini_6.pdf *History of Higashiizu Town*, Chapter 6, p. 360] - Higashiizu Town. In addition, ridgepole inscriptions from the 2nd year of {{ill|lt=Jōwa|Q1146143|WD=Q1146143}} ({{ill|lt=1346|Q6080|WD=Q6080}}) record the name '''Shirada Ki(hama) Shrine''' (しらだき(はま)じんじゃ) (*Zōtei Tōshū Shikō*). As indicated by its former shrine name and the enshrined deity {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Isotakeru-no-Mikoto|UNKNOWN}}, during the Edo period it also strongly exhibited the character of a Kinomiya Shrine associated with {{ill|lt=Kinomiya faith|Q11296898|WD=Q11296898}}. Even today, like {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Kawazu Kinomiya Shrine|UNKNOWN}} ({{ill|lt=Sugikubetsu-no-Mikoto Shrine|Q11522170|WD=Q11522170}}) in {{ill|lt=Kawazu Town|Q1203861|WD=Q1203861}}, it retains the cultural tradition known as “{{ill|lt=Bird abstinence and sake abstinence of Kawazu|Q11553983|WD=Q11553983|lt=bird abstinence and sake abstinence}}”On-site shrine history plaque[https://www.at-s.com/facilities/article/view/temple/137787.html Kinomiya Shrine (Higashiizu Town)] - Shizuoka Shimbun. === Enshrined deities === * Shiritaogi-no-Mikoto * {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Isotakeru-no-Mikoto|UNKNOWN}} === Notes === {{Reflist}} === External links === * [http://www.shizuoka-jinjacho.or.jp/shokai/jinja.php?id=4401004 Shiritaogi Shrine] - Shizuoka Prefecture Association of Shinto Shrines {{DEFAULTSORT:Shiritaogijinja}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Shiritaogi Shrine]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Shiritaogi Shrine]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shikinai Shōsha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Izu Province]] [[Category:pages]] [[Category:静岡県の神社]] [[Category:東伊豆町の歴史]] [[Category:東伊豆町の建築物]] [[Category:伊豆国の式内社]]

said to be the same as (P460) == * {{ill|Shiritakino Shrine|lt=Shiritakino Shrine|WD=Q135039656}} == street address (P6375) == * {'text': '東京都三宅村神着', 'language': 'ja'} [[JA:志理太乎宜神社]] {{wikidata link|Q134892415}} == Japanese content == {{Shrine |Name = Shiritaogi Shrine |Image = {{Image wanted|cat=Kamo District}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=14|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=250|frame-height=250|coord={{coord|34|48|8.3|N|139|3|33.8|E}}}} |Location = {{ill|lt=Shizuoka Prefecture|Q131320|WD=Q131320}}{{ill|lt=Kamo District|Q961511|WD=Q961511}}{{ill|lt=Higashiizu Town|Q304491|WD=Q304491}} Shirada 168 |Coordinates = {{Wiki coordinates DMS|34|48|8.3|N|139|3|33.8|E|display=inline,title}} |Enshrined deities = Shiritaogi-no-Mikoto, {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Isotakeru-no-Mikoto|1=UNKNOWN}} |Shrine rank = Former {{ill|lt=village shrine|Q134917284|WD=Q134917284}}, candidate {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=UNKNOWN}} |Founded = |Main hall = |Other names = Shirada Ki(hama) Shrine, (Shirada) Kinomiya Shrine |Pilgrimage status etc. = |Annual festival = October 24–25 |Rituals = |Map = {{Location map|Japan Shizuoka|label=Shirita
ogi Shrine|coordinates= {{coord2|34|48|8.3|N|139|3|33.8|E|}}|position=left|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} }} '''Shiritaogi Shrine''' (しりたおぎじんじゃ) is a {{ill|lt=Shinto shrine|Q845945|WD=Q845945}} located in Shirada, {{ill|lt=Higashiizu Town|Q304491|WD=Q304491}}, {{ill|lt=Kamo District|Q961511|WD=Q961511}}, {{ill|lt=Shizuoka Prefecture|Q131320|WD=Q131320}}[http://www.shizuoka-jinjacho.or.jp/shokai/jinja.php?id=4401004 Shiritaogi Shrine] - Shizuoka Prefecture Association of Shinto Shrines. During the Edo period it was called '''Kinomiya Shrine''' (きのみやじんじゃ), and this name is still used today as an alternative name, appearing on items such as the plaque on the torii gate[https://kotobank.jp/word/白田村-3062457 What is Shirada Village] - Kotobank[http://engishiki.org/izu/bun/iz140133-02.html Shiritaogi Shrine] - Survey of Engishiki Shrines. === Overview === The shrine is located immediately west of {{ill|lt=Katase-Shirata Station|Q6375267|WD=Q6375267}}, along {{ill|lt=National Route 135|Q6156779|WD=Q6156779}}. Together with {{ill|lt=Shiitore Shrine|Q134930749|WD=Q134930749}} on {{ill|lt=Miyakejima|Q1075076|WD=Q1075076}}, it is regarded as a candidate shrine (ronsha) of '''Shirita(ogi) Shrine''' (しりた(お)き[ぎ]じんじゃ), a {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=UNKNOWN}} that enshrines Shirita(ogi)-no-Mikoto, one of the princes of {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Mishima Daimyōjin|1=UNKNOWN}} ({{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Kotoshironushi-no-Kami|1=UNKNOWN}}). Meanwhile, {{ill|lt=Katasuge Shrine (Higashiizu Town)|Q134892414|WD=Q134892414|lt=Katasuge Shrine}} in the neighboring Katase district to the north—thought to have been founded around the same time—also serves as a candidate Shikinaisha enshrining another prince of Mishima Daimyōjin (Kotoshironushi-no-Kami) of Miyakejima, competing with {{ill|lt=Katasuge Shrine|Q134930291|WD=Q134930291}} on Miyakejima itself. Because of this branch-shrine–like relationship, it is possible that these shrines were originally established as refugee branch shrines or distant worship sites relocated from Miyakejima to avoid volcanic eruptions[http://engishiki.org/izu/bun/iz140116-04.html Katasuge Shrine] - Survey of Engishiki Shrines. Although the date of foundation is unknown, a record of reconstruction in the 2nd year of {{ill|lt=Kōei|Q980266|WD=Q980266}} ({{ill|lt=1343|Q6072|WD=Q6072}}) appears in the *Nantō Fudoki*[https://www.town.higashiizu.shizuoka.jp/bg/life/upload/kyo_higashiizutyousi_30mini_6.pdf *History of Higashiizu Town*, Chapter 6, p. 360] - Higashiizu Town. In addition, ridgepole inscriptions from the 2nd year of {{ill|lt=Jōwa|Q1146143|WD=Q1146143}} ({{ill|lt=1346|Q6080|WD=Q6080}}) record the name '''Shirada Ki(hama) Shrine''' (しらだき(はま)じんじゃ) (*Zōtei Tōshū Shikō*). As indicated by its former shrine name and the enshrined deity {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Isotakeru-no-Mikoto|1=UNKNOWN}}, during the Edo period it also strongly exhibited the character of a Kinomiya Shrine associated with {{ill|lt=Kinomiya faith|Q11296898|WD=Q11296898}}. Even today, like {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Kawazu Kinomiya Shrine|1=UNKNOWN}} ({{ill|lt=Sugikubetsu-no-Mikoto Shrine|Q11522170|WD=Q11522170}}) in {{ill|lt=Kawazu Town|Q1203861|WD=Q1203861}}, it retains the cultural tradition known as “{{ill|lt=Bird abstinence and sake abstinence of Kawazu|Q11553983|WD=Q11553983|lt=bird abstinence and sake abstinence}}”On-site shrine history plaque[https://www.at-s.com/facilities/article/view/temple/137787.html Kinomiya Shrine (Higashiizu Town)] - Shizuoka Shimbun. === Enshrined deities === * Shiritaogi-no-Mikoto * {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Isotakeru-no-Mikoto|1=UNKNOWN}} === Notes === {{Reflist}} === External links === * [http://www.shizuoka-jinjacho.or.jp/shokai/jinja.php?id=4401004 Shiritaogi Shrine] - Shizuoka Prefecture Association of Shinto Shrines {{DEFAULTSORT:Shiritaogijinja}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Shiritaogi Shrine]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Shiritaogi Shrine]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shikinai Shōsha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Izu Province]] [[Category:pages]] [[Category:静岡県の神社]] [[Category:東伊豆町の歴史]] [[Category:東伊豆町の建築物]] [[Category:伊豆国の式内社]]

said to be the same as (P460) == * {{ill|Shiritakino Shrine|lt=Shiritakino Shrine|WD=Q135039656}} == street address (P6375) == * {'text': '東京都三宅村神着', 'language': 'ja'} [[JA:志理太乎宜神社]] {{wikidata link|Q134892415}} == Japanese content == {{Shrine |Name = Shiritaogi Shrine |Image = {{Image wanted|cat=Kamo District}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=14|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=250|frame-height=250|coord={{coord|34|48|8.3|N|139|3|33.8|E}}}} |Location = {{ill|lt=Shizuoka Prefecture|Q131320|WD=Q131320}}{{ill|lt=Kamo District|Q961511|WD=Q961511}}{{ill|lt=Higashiizu Town|Q304491|WD=Q304491}} Shirada 168 |Coordinates = {{Wiki coordinates DMS|34|48|8.3|N|139|3|33.8|E|display=inline,title}} |Enshrined deities = Shiritaogi-no-Mikoto, {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Isotakeru-no-Mikoto|1=UNKNOWN}} |Shrine rank = Former {{ill|lt=village shrine|Q134917284|WD=Q134917284}}, candidate {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=UNKNOWN|WD=Q134917286}} |Founded = |Main hall = |Other names = Shirada Ki(hama) Shrine, (Shirada) Kinomiya Shrine |Pilgrimage status etc. = |Annual festival = October 24–25 |Rituals = |Map = {{Location map|Japan Shizuoka|label=Shirita
ogi Shrine|coordinates= {{coord2|34|48|8.3|N|139|3|33.8|E|}}|position=left|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} }} '''Shiritaogi Shrine''' (しりたおぎじんじゃ) is a {{ill|lt=Shinto shrine|Q845945|WD=Q845945}} located in Shirada, {{ill|lt=Higashiizu Town|Q304491|WD=Q304491}}, {{ill|lt=Kamo District|Q961511|WD=Q961511}}, {{ill|lt=Shizuoka Prefecture|Q131320|WD=Q131320}}[http://www.shizuoka-jinjacho.or.jp/shokai/jinja.php?id=4401004 Shiritaogi Shrine] - Shizuoka Prefecture Association of Shinto Shrines. During the Edo period it was called '''Kinomiya Shrine''' (きのみやじんじゃ), and this name is still used today as an alternative name, appearing on items such as the plaque on the torii gate[https://kotobank.jp/word/白田村-3062457 What is Shirada Village] - Kotobank[http://engishiki.org/izu/bun/iz140133-02.html Shiritaogi Shrine] - Survey of Engishiki Shrines. === Overview === The shrine is located immediately west of {{ill|lt=Katase-Shirata Station|Q6375267|WD=Q6375267}}, along {{ill|lt=National Route 135|Q6156779|WD=Q6156779}}. Together with {{ill|lt=Shiitore Shrine|Q134930749|WD=Q134930749}} on {{ill|lt=Miyakejima|Q1075076|WD=Q1075076}}, it is regarded as a candidate shrine (ronsha) of '''Shirita(ogi) Shrine''' (しりた(お)き[ぎ]じんじゃ), a {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=UNKNOWN|WD=Q134917286}} that enshrines Shirita(ogi)-no-Mikoto, one of the princes of {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Mishima Daimyōjin|1=UNKNOWN}} ({{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Kotoshironushi-no-Kami|1=UNKNOWN}}). Meanwhile, {{ill|lt=Katasuge Shrine (Higashiizu Town)|Q134892414|WD=Q134892414|lt=Katasuge Shrine}} in the neighboring Katase district to the north—thought to have been founded around the same time—also serves as a candidate Shikinaisha enshrining another prince of Mishima Daimyōjin (Kotoshironushi-no-Kami) of Miyakejima, competing with {{ill|lt=Katasuge Shrine|Q134930291|WD=Q134930291}} on Miyakejima itself. Because of this branch-shrine–like relationship, it is possible that these shrines were originally established as refugee branch shrines or distant worship sites relocated from Miyakejima to avoid volcanic eruptions[http://engishiki.org/izu/bun/iz140116-04.html Katasuge Shrine] - Survey of Engishiki Shrines. Although the date of foundation is unknown, a record of reconstruction in the 2nd year of {{ill|lt=Kōei|Q980266|WD=Q980266}} ({{ill|lt=1343|Q6072|WD=Q6072}}) appears in the *Nantō Fudoki*[https://www.town.higashiizu.shizuoka.jp/bg/life/upload/kyo_higashiizutyousi_30mini_6.pdf *History of Higashiizu Town*, Chapter 6, p. 360] - Higashiizu Town. In addition, ridgepole inscriptions from the 2nd year of {{ill|lt=Jōwa|Q1146143|WD=Q1146143}} ({{ill|lt=1346|Q6080|WD=Q6080}}) record the name '''Shirada Ki(hama) Shrine''' (しらだき(はま)じんじゃ) (*Zōtei Tōshū Shikō*). As indicated by its former shrine name and the enshrined deity {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Isotakeru-no-Mikoto|1=UNKNOWN}}, during the Edo period it also strongly exhibited the character of a Kinomiya Shrine associated with {{ill|lt=Kinomiya faith|Q11296898|WD=Q11296898}}. Even today, like {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Kawazu Kinomiya Shrine|1=UNKNOWN}} ({{ill|lt=Sugikubetsu-no-Mikoto Shrine|Q11522170|WD=Q11522170}}) in {{ill|lt=Kawazu Town|Q1203861|WD=Q1203861}}, it retains the cultural tradition known as “{{ill|lt=Bird abstinence and sake abstinence of Kawazu|Q11553983|WD=Q11553983|lt=bird abstinence and sake abstinence}}”On-site shrine history plaque[https://www.at-s.com/facilities/article/view/temple/137787.html Kinomiya Shrine (Higashiizu Town)] - Shizuoka Shimbun. === Enshrined deities === * Shiritaogi-no-Mikoto * {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Isotakeru-no-Mikoto|1=UNKNOWN}} === Notes === {{Reflist}} === External links === * [http://www.shizuoka-jinjacho.or.jp/shokai/jinja.php?id=4401004 Shiritaogi Shrine] - Shizuoka Prefecture Association of Shinto Shrines {{DEFAULTSORT:Shiritaogijinja}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Shiritaogi Shrine]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Shiritaogi Shrine]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shikinai Shōsha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Izu Province]] [[Category:pages]] [[Category:静岡県の神社]] [[Category:東伊豆町の歴史]] [[Category:東伊豆町の建築物]] [[Category:伊豆国の式内社]]

said to be the same as (P460) == * {{ill|Shiritakino Shrine|lt=Shiritakino Shrine|WD=Q135039656}} == street address (P6375) == * {'text': '東京都三宅村神着', 'language': 'ja'} [[JA:志理太乎宜神社]] {{wikidata link|Q134892415}} == Japanese content == {{Shrine |Name = Shiritaogi Shrine |Image = {{Image wanted|cat=Kamo District}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=14|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=250|frame-height=250|coord={{coord|34|48|8.3|N|139|3|33.8|E}}}} |Location = {{ill|lt=Shizuoka Prefecture|Q131320|WD=Q131320}}{{ill|lt=Kamo District|Q961511|WD=Q961511}}{{ill|lt=Higashiizu Town|Q304491|WD=Q304491}} Shirada 168 |Coordinates = {{Wiki coordinates DMS|34|48|8.3|N|139|3|33.8|E|display=inline,title}} |Enshrined deities = Shiritaogi-no-Mikoto, {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Isotakeru-no-Mikoto|1=UNKNOWN}} |Shrine rank = Former {{ill|lt=village shrine|Q134917284|WD=Q134917284}}, candidate {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=UNKNOWN|WD=Q134917286}} |Founded = |Main hall = |Other names = Shirada Ki(hama) Shrine, (Shirada) Kinomiya Shrine |Pilgrimage status etc. = |Annual festival = October 24–25 |Rituals = |Map = {{Location map|Japan Shizuoka|label=Shirita
ogi Shrine|coordinates= {{coord2|34|48|8.3|N|139|3|33.8|E|}}|position=left|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} }} '''Shiritaogi Shrine''' (しりたおぎじんじゃ) is a {{ill|lt=Shinto shrine|Q845945|WD=Q845945}} located in Shirada, {{ill|lt=Higashiizu Town|Q304491|WD=Q304491}}, {{ill|lt=Kamo District|Q961511|WD=Q961511}}, {{ill|lt=Shizuoka Prefecture|Q131320|WD=Q131320}}[http://www.shizuoka-jinjacho.or.jp/shokai/jinja.php?id=4401004 Shiritaogi Shrine] - Shizuoka Prefecture Association of Shinto Shrines. During the Edo period it was called '''Kinomiya Shrine''' (きのみやじんじゃ), and this name is still used today as an alternative name, appearing on items such as the plaque on the torii gate[https://kotobank.jp/word/白田村-3062457 What is Shirada Village] - Kotobank[http://engishiki.org/izu/bun/iz140133-02.html Shiritaogi Shrine] - Survey of Engishiki Shrines. === Overview === The shrine is located immediately west of {{ill|lt=Katase-Shirata Station|Q6375267|WD=Q6375267}}, along {{ill|lt=National Route 135|Q6156779|WD=Q6156779}}. Together with {{ill|lt=Shiitore Shrine|Q134930749|WD=Q134930749}} on {{ill|lt=Miyakejima|Q1075076|WD=Q1075076}}, it is regarded as a candidate shrine (ronsha) of '''Shirita(ogi) Shrine''' (しりた(お)き[ぎ]じんじゃ), a {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=UNKNOWN|WD=Q134917286}} that enshrines Shirita(ogi)-no-Mikoto, one of the princes of {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Mishima Daimyōjin|1=UNKNOWN}} ({{ill|UNKNOWN|WD=Q8009611|lt=Kotoshironushi-no-Kami|1=UNKNOWN}}). Meanwhile, {{ill|lt=Katasuge Shrine (Higashiizu Town)|Q134892414|WD=Q134892414|lt=Katasuge Shrine}} in the neighboring Katase district to the north—thought to have been founded around the same time—also serves as a candidate Shikinaisha enshrining another prince of Mishima Daimyōjin (Kotoshironushi-no-Kami) of Miyakejima, competing with {{ill|lt=Katasuge Shrine|Q134930291|WD=Q134930291}} on Miyakejima itself. Because of this branch-shrine–like relationship, it is possible that these shrines were originally established as refugee branch shrines or distant worship sites relocated from Miyakejima to avoid volcanic eruptions[http://engishiki.org/izu/bun/iz140116-04.html Katasuge Shrine] - Survey of Engishiki Shrines. Although the date of foundation is unknown, a record of reconstruction in the 2nd year of {{ill|lt=Kōei|Q980266|WD=Q980266}} ({{ill|lt=1343|Q6072|WD=Q6072}}) appears in the *Nantō Fudoki*[https://www.town.higashiizu.shizuoka.jp/bg/life/upload/kyo_higashiizutyousi_30mini_6.pdf *History of Higashiizu Town*, Chapter 6, p. 360] - Higashiizu Town. In addition, ridgepole inscriptions from the 2nd year of {{ill|lt=Jōwa|Q1146143|WD=Q1146143}} ({{ill|lt=1346|Q6080|WD=Q6080}}) record the name '''Shirada Ki(hama) Shrine''' (しらだき(はま)じんじゃ) (*Zōtei Tōshū Shikō*). As indicated by its former shrine name and the enshrined deity {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Isotakeru-no-Mikoto|1=UNKNOWN}}, during the Edo period it also strongly exhibited the character of a Kinomiya Shrine associated with {{ill|lt=Kinomiya faith|Q11296898|WD=Q11296898}}. Even today, like {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Kawazu Kinomiya Shrine|1=UNKNOWN}} ({{ill|lt=Sugikubetsu-no-Mikoto Shrine|Q11522170|WD=Q11522170}}) in {{ill|lt=Kawazu Town|Q1203861|WD=Q1203861}}, it retains the cultural tradition known as “{{ill|lt=Bird abstinence and sake abstinence of Kawazu|Q11553983|WD=Q11553983|lt=bird abstinence and sake abstinence}}”On-site shrine history plaque[https://www.at-s.com/facilities/article/view/temple/137787.html Kinomiya Shrine (Higashiizu Town)] - Shizuoka Shimbun. === Enshrined deities === * Shiritaogi-no-Mikoto * {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Isotakeru-no-Mikoto|1=UNKNOWN}} === Notes === {{Reflist}} === External links === * [http://www.shizuoka-jinjacho.or.jp/shokai/jinja.php?id=4401004 Shiritaogi Shrine] - Shizuoka Prefecture Association of Shinto Shrines {{DEFAULTSORT:Shiritaogijinja}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Shiritaogi Shrine]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Shiritaogi Shrine]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shikinai Shōsha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Izu Province]] [[Category:pages]] [[Category:静岡県の神社]] [[Category:東伊豆町の歴史]] [[Category:東伊豆町の建築物]] [[Category:伊豆国の式内社]]

said to be the same as (P460) == * {{ill|Shiritakino Shrine|lt=Shiritakino Shrine|WD=Q135039656}} == street address (P6375) == * {'text': '東京都三宅村神着', 'language': 'ja'} [[JA:志理太乎宜神社]] {{wikidata link|Q134892415}} == Japanese content == {{Shrine |Name = Shiritaogi Shrine |Image = {{Image wanted|cat=Kamo District}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=14|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=250|frame-height=250|coord={{coord|34|48|8.3|N|139|3|33.8|E}}}} |Location = {{ill|lt=Shizuoka Prefecture|Q131320|WD=Q131320}}{{ill|lt=Kamo District|Q961511|WD=Q961511}}{{ill|lt=Higashiizu Town|Q304491|WD=Q304491}} Shirada 168 |Coordinates = {{Wiki coordinates DMS|34|48|8.3|N|139|3|33.8|E|display=inline,title}} |Enshrined deities = Shiritaogi-no-Mikoto, {{ill|UNKNOWN|WD=Q10881556|lt=Isotakeru-no-Mikoto|1=UNKNOWN}} |Shrine rank = Former {{ill|lt=village shrine|Q134917284|WD=Q134917284}}, candidate {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=UNKNOWN|WD=Q134917286}} |Founded = |Main hall = |Other names = Shirada Ki(hama) Shrine, (Shirada) Kinomiya Shrine |Pilgrimage status etc. = |Annual festival = October 24–25 |Rituals = |Map = {{Location map|Japan Shizuoka|label=Shirita
ogi Shrine|coordinates= {{coord2|34|48|8.3|N|139|3|33.8|E|}}|position=left|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} }} '''Shiritaogi Shrine''' (しりたおぎじんじゃ) is a {{ill|lt=Shinto shrine|Q845945|WD=Q845945}} located in Shirada, {{ill|lt=Higashiizu Town|Q304491|WD=Q304491}}, {{ill|lt=Kamo District|Q961511|WD=Q961511}}, {{ill|lt=Shizuoka Prefecture|Q131320|WD=Q131320}}[http://www.shizuoka-jinjacho.or.jp/shokai/jinja.php?id=4401004 Shiritaogi Shrine] - Shizuoka Prefecture Association of Shinto Shrines. During the Edo period it was called '''Kinomiya Shrine''' (きのみやじんじゃ), and this name is still used today as an alternative name, appearing on items such as the plaque on the torii gate[https://kotobank.jp/word/白田村-3062457 What is Shirada Village] - Kotobank[http://engishiki.org/izu/bun/iz140133-02.html Shiritaogi Shrine] - Survey of Engishiki Shrines. === Overview === The shrine is located immediately west of {{ill|lt=Katase-Shirata Station|Q6375267|WD=Q6375267}}, along {{ill|lt=National Route 135|Q6156779|WD=Q6156779}}. Together with {{ill|lt=Shiitore Shrine|Q134930749|WD=Q134930749}} on {{ill|lt=Miyakejima|Q1075076|WD=Q1075076}}, it is regarded as a candidate shrine (ronsha) of '''Shirita(ogi) Shrine''' (しりた(お)き[ぎ]じんじゃ), a {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=UNKNOWN|WD=Q134917286}} that enshrines Shirita(ogi)-no-Mikoto, one of the princes of {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Mishima Daimyōjin|1=UNKNOWN}} ({{ill|UNKNOWN|WD=Q8009611|lt=Kotoshironushi-no-Kami|1=UNKNOWN}}). Meanwhile, {{ill|lt=Katasuge Shrine (Higashiizu Town)|Q134892414|WD=Q134892414|lt=Katasuge Shrine}} in the neighboring Katase district to the north—thought to have been founded around the same time—also serves as a candidate Shikinaisha enshrining another prince of Mishima Daimyōjin (Kotoshironushi-no-Kami) of Miyakejima, competing with {{ill|lt=Katasuge Shrine|Q134930291|WD=Q134930291}} on Miyakejima itself. Because of this branch-shrine–like relationship, it is possible that these shrines were originally established as refugee branch shrines or distant worship sites relocated from Miyakejima to avoid volcanic eruptions[http://engishiki.org/izu/bun/iz140116-04.html Katasuge Shrine] - Survey of Engishiki Shrines. Although the date of foundation is unknown, a record of reconstruction in the 2nd year of {{ill|lt=Kōei|Q980266|WD=Q980266}} ({{ill|lt=1343|Q6072|WD=Q6072}}) appears in the *Nantō Fudoki*[https://www.town.higashiizu.shizuoka.jp/bg/life/upload/kyo_higashiizutyousi_30mini_6.pdf *History of Higashiizu Town*, Chapter 6, p. 360] - Higashiizu Town. In addition, ridgepole inscriptions from the 2nd year of {{ill|lt=Jōwa|Q1146143|WD=Q1146143}} ({{ill|lt=1346|Q6080|WD=Q6080}}) record the name '''Shirada Ki(hama) Shrine''' (しらだき(はま)じんじゃ) (*Zōtei Tōshū Shikō*). As indicated by its former shrine name and the enshrined deity {{ill|UNKNOWN|WD=Q10881556|lt=Isotakeru-no-Mikoto|1=UNKNOWN}}, during the Edo period it also strongly exhibited the character of a Kinomiya Shrine associated with {{ill|lt=Kinomiya faith|Q11296898|WD=Q11296898}}. Even today, like {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Kawazu Kinomiya Shrine|1=UNKNOWN}} ({{ill|lt=Sugikubetsu-no-Mikoto Shrine|Q11522170|WD=Q11522170}}) in {{ill|lt=Kawazu Town|Q1203861|WD=Q1203861}}, it retains the cultural tradition known as “{{ill|lt=Bird abstinence and sake abstinence of Kawazu|Q11553983|WD=Q11553983|lt=bird abstinence and sake abstinence}}”On-site shrine history plaque[https://www.at-s.com/facilities/article/view/temple/137787.html Kinomiya Shrine (Higashiizu Town)] - Shizuoka Shimbun. === Enshrined deities === * Shiritaogi-no-Mikoto * {{ill|UNKNOWN|WD=Q10881556|lt=Isotakeru-no-Mikoto|1=UNKNOWN}} === Notes === {{Reflist}} === External links === * [http://www.shizuoka-jinjacho.or.jp/shokai/jinja.php?id=4401004 Shiritaogi Shrine] - Shizuoka Prefecture Association of Shinto Shrines {{DEFAULTSORT:Shiritaogijinja}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Shiritaogi Shrine]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Shiritaogi Shrine]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shikinai Shōsha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Izu Province]] [[Category:pages]] [[Category:静岡県の神社]] [[Category:東伊豆町の歴史]] [[Category:東伊豆町の建築物]] [[Category:伊豆国の式内社]]
