said to be the same as (P460) == * {{ill|Shiritakino Shrine|lt=Shiritakino Shrine|WD=Q135039656}} == street address (P6375) == * {'text': '東京都三宅村神着', 'language': 'ja'} [[JA:志理太乎宜神社]] {{wikidata link|Q134892415}} == Japanese content == {{Shrine |Name = Shiritaogi Shrine |Image = {{Image wanted|cat=Kamo District}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=14|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=250|frame-height=250|coord={{coord|34|48|8.3|N|139|3|33.8|E}}}} |Location = {{ill|lt=Shizuoka Prefecture|Q131320|WD=Q131320}}{{ill|lt=Kamo District|Q961511|WD=Q961511}}{{ill|lt=Higashiizu Town|Q304491|WD=Q304491}} Shirada 168 |Coordinates = {{Wiki coordinates DMS|34|48|8.3|N|139|3|33.8|E|display=inline,title}} |Enshrined deities = Shiritaogi-no-Mikoto, {{ill|UNKNOWN|WD=Q10881556|lt=Isotakeru-no-Mikoto|1=UNKNOWN}} |Shrine rank = Former {{ill|lt=village shrine|Q134917284|WD=Q134917284}}, candidate {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=UNKNOWN|WD=Q134917286}} |Founded = |Main hall = |Other names = Shirada Ki(hama) Shrine, (Shirada) Kinomiya Shrine |Pilgrimage status etc. = |Annual festival = October 24–25 |Rituals = |Map = {{Location map|Japan Shizuoka|label=Shirita
ogi Shrine|coordinates= {{coord2|34|48|8.3|N|139|3|33.8|E|}}|position=left|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} }} '''Shiritaogi Shrine''' (しりたおぎじんじゃ) is a {{ill|lt=Shinto shrine|Q845945|WD=Q845945}} located in Shirada, {{ill|lt=Higashiizu Town|Q304491|WD=Q304491}}, {{ill|lt=Kamo District|Q961511|WD=Q961511}}, {{ill|lt=Shizuoka Prefecture|Q131320|WD=Q131320}}[http://www.shizuoka-jinjacho.or.jp/shokai/jinja.php?id=4401004 Shiritaogi Shrine] - Shizuoka Prefecture Association of Shinto Shrines. During the Edo period it was called '''Kinomiya Shrine''' (きのみやじんじゃ), and this name is still used today as an alternative name, appearing on items such as the plaque on the torii gate[https://kotobank.jp/word/白田村-3062457 What is Shirada Village] - Kotobank[http://engishiki.org/izu/bun/iz140133-02.html Shiritaogi Shrine] - Survey of Engishiki Shrines. === Overview === The shrine is located immediately west of {{ill|lt=Katase-Shirata Station|Q6375267|WD=Q6375267}}, along {{ill|lt=National Route 135|Q6156779|WD=Q6156779}}. Together with {{ill|lt=Shiitore Shrine|Q134930749|WD=Q134930749}} on {{ill|lt=Miyakejima|Q1075076|WD=Q1075076}}, it is regarded as a candidate shrine (ronsha) of '''Shirita(ogi) Shrine''' (しりた(お)き[ぎ]じんじゃ), a {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=UNKNOWN|WD=Q134917286}} that enshrines Shirita(ogi)-no-Mikoto, one of the princes of {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Mishima Daimyōjin|1=UNKNOWN}} ({{ill|UNKNOWN|WD=Q8009611|lt=Kotoshironushi-no-Kami|1=UNKNOWN}}). Meanwhile, {{ill|lt=Katasuge Shrine (Higashiizu Town)|Q134892414|WD=Q134892414|lt=Katasuge Shrine}} in the neighboring Katase district to the north—thought to have been founded around the same time—also serves as a candidate Shikinaisha enshrining another prince of Mishima Daimyōjin (Kotoshironushi-no-Kami) of Miyakejima, competing with {{ill|lt=Katasuge Shrine|Q134930291|WD=Q134930291}} on Miyakejima itself. Because of this branch-shrine–like relationship, it is possible that these shrines were originally established as refugee branch shrines or distant worship sites relocated from Miyakejima to avoid volcanic eruptions[http://engishiki.org/izu/bun/iz140116-04.html Katasuge Shrine] - Survey of Engishiki Shrines. Although the date of foundation is unknown, a record of reconstruction in the 2nd year of {{ill|lt=Kōei|Q980266|WD=Q980266}} ({{ill|lt=1343|Q6072|WD=Q6072}}) appears in the *Nantō Fudoki*[https://www.town.higashiizu.shizuoka.jp/bg/life/upload/kyo_higashiizutyousi_30mini_6.pdf *History of Higashiizu Town*, Chapter 6, p. 360] - Higashiizu Town. In addition, ridgepole inscriptions from the 2nd year of {{ill|lt=Jōwa|Q1146143|WD=Q1146143}} ({{ill|lt=1346|Q6080|WD=Q6080}}) record the name '''Shirada Ki(hama) Shrine''' (しらだき(はま)じんじゃ) (*Zōtei Tōshū Shikō*). As indicated by its former shrine name and the enshrined deity {{ill|UNKNOWN|WD=Q10881556|lt=Isotakeru-no-Mikoto|1=UNKNOWN}}, during the Edo period it also strongly exhibited the character of a Kinomiya Shrine associated with {{ill|lt=Kinomiya faith|Q11296898|WD=Q11296898}}. Even today, like {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Kawazu Kinomiya Shrine|1=UNKNOWN}} ({{ill|lt=Sugikubetsu-no-Mikoto Shrine|Q11522170|WD=Q11522170}}) in {{ill|lt=Kawazu Town|Q1203861|WD=Q1203861}}, it retains the cultural tradition known as “{{ill|lt=Bird abstinence and sake abstinence of Kawazu|Q11553983|WD=Q11553983|lt=bird abstinence and sake abstinence}}”On-site shrine history plaque[https://www.at-s.com/facilities/article/view/temple/137787.html Kinomiya Shrine (Higashiizu Town)] - Shizuoka Shimbun. === Enshrined deities === * Shiritaogi-no-Mikoto * {{ill|UNKNOWN|WD=Q10881556|lt=Isotakeru-no-Mikoto|1=UNKNOWN}} === Notes === {{Reflist}} === External links === * [http://www.shizuoka-jinjacho.or.jp/shokai/jinja.php?id=4401004 Shiritaogi Shrine] - Shizuoka Prefecture Association of Shinto Shrines {{DEFAULTSORT:Shiritaogijinja}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Shiritaogi Shrine]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Shiritaogi Shrine]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shikinai Shōsha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Izu Province]] [[Category:pages]] [[Category:静岡県の神社]] [[Category:東伊豆町の歴史]] [[Category:東伊豆町の建築物]] [[Category:伊豆国の式内社]]

== said to be the same as (P460) == * {{ill|Shiritakino Shrine|lt=Shiritakino Shrine|WD=Q135039656|1=Shiritakino Shrine}} == street address (P6375) == * {'text': '東京都三宅村神着', 'language': 'ja'} [[JA:志理太乎宜神社]] {{wikidata link|Q134892415}} == Japanese content == {{Shrine |Name = Shiritaogi Shrine |Image = {{Image wanted|cat=Kamo District}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=14|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=250|frame-height=250|coord={{coord|34|48|8.3|N|139|3|33.8|E}}}} |Location = {{ill|lt=Shizuoka Prefecture|Q131320|WD=Q131320|1=Shizuoka Prefecture}}{{ill|lt=Kamo District|Q961511|WD=Q961511|1=Kamo District, Shizuoka}}{{ill|lt=Higashiizu Town|Q304491|WD=Q304491|1=Higashiizu}} Shirada 168 |Coordinates = {{Wiki coordinates DMS|34|48|8.3|N|139|3|33.8|E|display=inline,title}} |Enshrined deities = Shiritaogi-no-Mikoto, {{ill|UNKNOWN|WD=Q10881556|lt=Isotakeru-no-Mikoto|1=Isotakeru-no-Mikoto}} |Shrine rank = Former {{ill|lt=village shrine|Q134917284|WD=Q134917284|1=Son-sha}}, candidate {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Shikinaisha|WD=Q134917286}} |Founded = |Main hall = |Other names = Shirada Ki(hama) Shrine, (Shirada) Kinomiya Shrine |Pilgrimage status etc. = |Annual festival = October 24–25 |Rituals = |Map = {{Location map|Japan Shizuoka|label=Shirita
ogi Shrine|coordinates= {{coord2|34|48|8.3|N|139|3|33.8|E|}}|position=left|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} }} '''Shiritaogi Shrine''' (しりたおぎじんじゃ) is a {{ill|lt=Shinto shrine|Q845945|WD=Q845945|1=Shinto shrine}} located in Shirada, {{ill|lt=Higashiizu Town|Q304491|WD=Q304491|1=Higashiizu}}, {{ill|lt=Kamo District|Q961511|WD=Q961511|1=Kamo District, Shizuoka}}, {{ill|lt=Shizuoka Prefecture|Q131320|WD=Q131320|1=Shizuoka Prefecture}}[http://www.shizuoka-jinjacho.or.jp/shokai/jinja.php?id=4401004 Shiritaogi Shrine] - Shizuoka Prefecture Association of Shinto Shrines. During the Edo period it was called '''Kinomiya Shrine''' (きのみやじんじゃ), and this name is still used today as an alternative name, appearing on items such as the plaque on the torii gate[https://kotobank.jp/word/白田村-3062457 What is Shirada Village] - Kotobank[http://engishiki.org/izu/bun/iz140133-02.html Shiritaogi Shrine] - Survey of Engishiki Shrines. === Overview === The shrine is located immediately west of {{ill|lt=Katase-Shirata Station|Q6375267|WD=Q6375267|1=Katase-Shirata Station}}, along {{ill|lt=National Route 135|Q6156779|WD=Q6156779|1=Japan National Route 135}}. Together with {{ill|lt=Shiitore Shrine|Q134930749|WD=Q134930749|1=Shiitori Shrine}} on {{ill|lt=Miyakejima|Q1075076|WD=Q1075076|1=Miyake-jima}}, it is regarded as a candidate shrine (ronsha) of '''Shirita(ogi) Shrine''' (しりた(お)き[ぎ]じんじゃ), a {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Shikinaisha|WD=Q134917286}} that enshrines Shirita(ogi)-no-Mikoto, one of the princes of {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Mishima Daimyōjin|1=UNKNOWN}} ({{ill|UNKNOWN|WD=Q8009611|lt=Kotoshironushi-no-Kami|1=Kotoshironushi-no-Mikoto}}). Meanwhile, {{ill|lt=Katasuge Shrine (Higashiizu Town)|Q134892414|WD=Q134892414|lt=Katasuge Shrine|1=Katasuge Shrine}} in the neighboring Katase district to the north—thought to have been founded around the same time—also serves as a candidate Shikinaisha enshrining another prince of Mishima Daimyōjin (Kotoshironushi-no-Kami) of Miyakejima, competing with {{ill|lt=Katasuge Shrine|Q134930291|WD=Q134930291|1=Katasuge Shrine}} on Miyakejima itself. Because of this branch-shrine–like relationship, it is possible that these shrines were originally established as refugee branch shrines or distant worship sites relocated from Miyakejima to avoid volcanic eruptions[http://engishiki.org/izu/bun/iz140116-04.html Katasuge Shrine] - Survey of Engishiki Shrines. Although the date of foundation is unknown, a record of reconstruction in the 2nd year of {{ill|lt=Kōei|Q980266|WD=Q980266|1=Kōei}} ({{ill|lt=1343|Q6072|WD=Q6072|1=1343}}) appears in the *Nantō Fudoki*[https://www.town.higashiizu.shizuoka.jp/bg/life/upload/kyo_higashiizutyousi_30mini_6.pdf *History of Higashiizu Town*, Chapter 6, p. 360] - Higashiizu Town. In addition, ridgepole inscriptions from the 2nd year of {{ill|lt=Jōwa|Q1146143|WD=Q1146143|1=Jōwa (Muromachi period)}} ({{ill|lt=1346|Q6080|WD=Q6080|1=1346}}) record the name '''Shirada Ki(hama) Shrine''' (しらだき(はま)じんじゃ) (*Zōtei Tōshū Shikō*). As indicated by its former shrine name and the enshrined deity {{ill|UNKNOWN|WD=Q10881556|lt=Isotakeru-no-Mikoto|1=Isotakeru-no-Mikoto}}, during the Edo period it also strongly exhibited the character of a Kinomiya Shrine associated with {{ill|lt=Kinomiya faith|Q11296898|WD=Q11296898|1=Kinomiya Faith}}. Even today, like {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Kawazu Kinomiya Shrine|1=UNKNOWN}} ({{ill|lt=Sugikubetsu-no-Mikoto Shrine|Q11522170|WD=Q11522170|1=Sugihokowake no Mikoto Shrine}}) in {{ill|lt=Kawazu Town|Q1203861|WD=Q1203861|1=Kawazu, Shizuoka}}, it retains the cultural tradition known as “{{ill|lt=Bird abstinence and sake abstinence of Kawazu|Q11553983|WD=Q11553983|lt=bird abstinence and sake abstinence|1=bird abstinence and sake abstinence}}”On-site shrine history plaque[https://www.at-s.com/facilities/article/view/temple/137787.html Kinomiya Shrine (Higashiizu Town)] - Shizuoka Shimbun. === Enshrined deities === * Shiritaogi-no-Mikoto * {{ill|UNKNOWN|WD=Q10881556|lt=Isotakeru-no-Mikoto|1=Isotakeru-no-Mikoto}} === Notes === {{Reflist}} === External links === * [http://www.shizuoka-jinjacho.or.jp/shokai/jinja.php?id=4401004 Shiritaogi Shrine] - Shizuoka Prefecture Association of Shinto Shrines {{DEFAULTSORT:Shiritaogijinja}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Shiritaogi Shrine]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Shiritaogi Shrine]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shikinai Shōsha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Izu Province]] [[Category:pages]] [[Category:静岡県の神社]] [[Category:東伊豆町の歴史]] [[Category:東伊豆町の建築物]] [[Category:伊豆国の式内社]]

== said to be the same as (P460) == * {{ill|Shiritakino Shrine|lt=Shiritakino Shrine|WD=Q135039656|1=Shiritakino Shrine}} == street address (P6375) == * {'text': '東京都三宅村神着', 'language': 'ja'} [[JA:志理太乎宜神社]] {{wikidata link|Q134892415}} == Japanese content == {{Shrine |Name = Shiritaogi Shrine |Image = {{Image wanted|cat=Kamo District}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=14|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=250|frame-height=250|coord={{coord|34|48|8.3|N|139|3|33.8|E}}}} |Location = {{ill|lt=Shizuoka Prefecture|Q131320|WD=Q131320|1=Shizuoka Prefecture}}{{ill|lt=Kamo District|Q961511|WD=Q961511|1=Kamo District, Shizuoka}}{{ill|lt=Higashiizu Town|Q304491|WD=Q304491|1=Higashiizu}} Shirada 168 |Coordinates = {{Wiki coordinates DMS|34|48|8.3|N|139|3|33.8|E|display=inline,title}} |Enshrined deities = Shiritaogi-no-Mikoto, {{ill|UNKNOWN|WD=Q10881556|lt=Isotakeru-no-Mikoto|1=Isotakeru-no-Mikoto}} |Shrine rank = Former {{ill|lt=village shrine|Q134917284|WD=Q134917284|1=Son-sha}}, candidate {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Shikinaisha|WD=Q134917286}} |Founded = |Main hall = |Other names = Shirada Ki(hama) Shrine, (Shirada) Kinomiya Shrine |Pilgrimage status etc. = |Annual festival = October 24–25 |Rituals = |Map = {{Location map|Japan Shizuoka|label=Shirita
ogi Shrine|coordinates= {{coord2|34|48|8.3|N|139|3|33.8|E|}}|position=left|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} }} '''Shiritaogi Shrine''' (しりたおぎじんじゃ) is a {{ill|lt=Shinto shrine|Q845945|WD=Q845945|1=Shinto shrine}} located in Shirada, {{ill|lt=Higashiizu Town|Q304491|WD=Q304491|1=Higashiizu}}, {{ill|lt=Kamo District|Q961511|WD=Q961511|1=Kamo District, Shizuoka}}, {{ill|lt=Shizuoka Prefecture|Q131320|WD=Q131320|1=Shizuoka Prefecture}}[http://www.shizuoka-jinjacho.or.jp/shokai/jinja.php?id=4401004 Shiritaogi Shrine] - Shizuoka Prefecture Association of Shinto Shrines. During the Edo period it was called '''Kinomiya Shrine''' (きのみやじんじゃ), and this name is still used today as an alternative name, appearing on items such as the plaque on the torii gate[https://kotobank.jp/word/白田村-3062457 What is Shirada Village] - Kotobank[http://engishiki.org/izu/bun/iz140133-02.html Shiritaogi Shrine] - Survey of Engishiki Shrines. === Overview === The shrine is located immediately west of {{ill|lt=Katase-Shirata Station|Q6375267|WD=Q6375267|1=Katase-Shirata Station}}, along {{ill|lt=National Route 135|Q6156779|WD=Q6156779|1=Japan National Route 135}}. Together with {{ill|lt=Shiitore Shrine|Q134930749|WD=Q134930749|1=Shiitori Shrine}} on {{ill|lt=Miyakejima|Q1075076|WD=Q1075076|1=Miyake-jima}}, it is regarded as a candidate shrine (ronsha) of '''Shirita(ogi) Shrine''' (しりた(お)き[ぎ]じんじゃ), a {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Shikinaisha|WD=Q134917286}} that enshrines Shirita(ogi)-no-Mikoto, one of the princes of {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Mishima Daimyōjin|1=UNKNOWN}} ({{ill|UNKNOWN|WD=Q8009611|lt=Kotoshironushi-no-Kami|1=Kotoshironushi-no-Mikoto}}). Meanwhile, {{ill|lt=Katasuge Shrine (Higashiizu Town)|Q134892414|WD=Q134892414|lt=Katasuge Shrine|1=Katasuge Shrine}} in the neighboring Katase district to the north—thought to have been founded around the same time—also serves as a candidate Shikinaisha enshrining another prince of Mishima Daimyōjin (Kotoshironushi-no-Kami) of Miyakejima, competing with {{ill|lt=Katasuge Shrine|Q134930291|WD=Q134930291|1=Katasuge Shrine}} on Miyakejima itself. Because of this branch-shrine–like relationship, it is possible that these shrines were originally established as refugee branch shrines or distant worship sites relocated from Miyakejima to avoid volcanic eruptions[http://engishiki.org/izu/bun/iz140116-04.html Katasuge Shrine] - Survey of Engishiki Shrines. Although the date of foundation is unknown, a record of reconstruction in the 2nd year of {{ill|lt=Kōei|Q980266|WD=Q980266|1=Kōei}} ({{ill|lt=1343|Q6072|WD=Q6072|1=1343}}) appears in the *Nantō Fudoki*[https://www.town.higashiizu.shizuoka.jp/bg/life/upload/kyo_higashiizutyousi_30mini_6.pdf *History of Higashiizu Town*, Chapter 6, p. 360] - Higashiizu Town. In addition, ridgepole inscriptions from the 2nd year of {{ill|lt=Jōwa|Q1146143|WD=Q1146143|1=Jōwa (Muromachi period)}} ({{ill|lt=1346|Q6080|WD=Q6080|1=1346}}) record the name '''Shirada Ki(hama) Shrine''' (しらだき(はま)じんじゃ) (*Zōtei Tōshū Shikō*). As indicated by its former shrine name and the enshrined deity {{ill|UNKNOWN|WD=Q10881556|lt=Isotakeru-no-Mikoto|1=Isotakeru-no-Mikoto}}, during the Edo period it also strongly exhibited the character of a Kinomiya Shrine associated with {{ill|lt=Kinomiya faith|Q11296898|WD=Q11296898|1=Kinomiya Faith}}. Even today, like {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Kawazu Kinomiya Shrine|1=UNKNOWN}} ({{ill|lt=Sugikubetsu-no-Mikoto Shrine|Q11522170|WD=Q11522170|1=Sugihokowake no Mikoto Shrine}}) in {{ill|lt=Kawazu Town|Q1203861|WD=Q1203861|1=Kawazu, Shizuoka}}, it retains the cultural tradition known as “{{ill|lt=Bird abstinence and sake abstinence of Kawazu|Q11553983|WD=Q11553983|lt=bird abstinence and sake abstinence|1=bird abstinence and sake abstinence}}”On-site shrine history plaque[https://www.at-s.com/facilities/article/view/temple/137787.html Kinomiya Shrine (Higashiizu Town)] - Shizuoka Shimbun. === Enshrined deities === * Shiritaogi-no-Mikoto * {{ill|UNKNOWN|WD=Q10881556|lt=Isotakeru-no-Mikoto|1=Isotakeru-no-Mikoto}} === Notes === {{Reflist}} === External links === * [http://www.shizuoka-jinjacho.or.jp/shokai/jinja.php?id=4401004 Shiritaogi Shrine] - Shizuoka Prefecture Association of Shinto Shrines {{DEFAULTSORT:Shiritaogijinja}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Shiritaogi Shrine]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Shiritaogi Shrine]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shikinai Shōsha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Izu Province]] [[Category:pages]] [[Category:静岡県の神社]] [[Category:東伊豆町の歴史]] [[Category:東伊豆町の建築物]] [[Category:伊豆国の式内社]]

== said to be the same as (P460) == * {{ill|Shiritakino Shrine|lt=Shiritakino Shrine|WD=Q135039656|1=Shiritakino Shrine}} == street address (P6375) == * {'text': '東京都三宅村神着', 'language': 'ja'} [[JA:志理太乎宜神社]] {{wikidata link|Q134892415}} == Japanese content == {{Shrine |Name = Shiritaogi Shrine |Image = {{Image wanted|cat=Kamo District}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=14|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=250|frame-height=250|coord={{coord|34|48|8.3|N|139|3|33.8|E}}}} |Location = {{ill|lt=Shizuoka Prefecture|Q131320|WD=Q131320|1=Shizuoka Prefecture}}{{ill|lt=Kamo District|Q961511|WD=Q961511|1=Kamo District, Shizuoka}}{{ill|lt=Higashiizu Town|Q304491|WD=Q304491|1=Higashiizu}} Shirada 168 |Coordinates = {{Wiki coordinates DMS|34|48|8.3|N|139|3|33.8|E|display=inline,title}} |Enshrined deities = Shiritaogi-no-Mikoto, {{ill|UNKNOWN|WD=Q10881556|lt=Isotakeru-no-Mikoto|1=Isotakeru-no-Mikoto}} |Shrine rank = Former {{ill|lt=village shrine|Q134917284|WD=Q134917284|1=Son-sha}}, candidate {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Shikinaisha|WD=Q134917286}} |Founded = |Main hall = |Other names = Shirada Ki(hama) Shrine, (Shirada) Kinomiya Shrine |Pilgrimage status etc. = |Annual festival = October 24–25 |Rituals = |Map = {{Location map|Japan Shizuoka|label=Shirita
ogi Shrine|coordinates= {{coord2|34|48|8.3|N|139|3|33.8|E|}}|position=left|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} }} '''Shiritaogi Shrine''' (しりたおぎじんじゃ) is a {{ill|lt=Shinto shrine|Q845945|WD=Q845945|1=Shinto shrine}} located in Shirada, {{ill|lt=Higashiizu Town|Q304491|WD=Q304491|1=Higashiizu}}, {{ill|lt=Kamo District|Q961511|WD=Q961511|1=Kamo District, Shizuoka}}, {{ill|lt=Shizuoka Prefecture|Q131320|WD=Q131320|1=Shizuoka Prefecture}}[http://www.shizuoka-jinjacho.or.jp/shokai/jinja.php?id=4401004 Shiritaogi Shrine] - Shizuoka Prefecture Association of Shinto Shrines. During the Edo period it was called '''Kinomiya Shrine''' (きのみやじんじゃ), and this name is still used today as an alternative name, appearing on items such as the plaque on the torii gate[https://kotobank.jp/word/白田村-3062457 What is Shirada Village] - Kotobank[http://engishiki.org/izu/bun/iz140133-02.html Shiritaogi Shrine] - Survey of Engishiki Shrines. === Overview === The shrine is located immediately west of {{ill|lt=Katase-Shirata Station|Q6375267|WD=Q6375267|1=Katase-Shirata Station}}, along {{ill|lt=National Route 135|Q6156779|WD=Q6156779|1=Japan National Route 135}}. Together with {{ill|lt=Shiitore Shrine|Q134930749|WD=Q134930749|1=Shiitori Shrine}} on {{ill|lt=Miyakejima|Q1075076|WD=Q1075076|1=Miyake-jima}}, it is regarded as a candidate shrine (ronsha) of '''Shirita(ogi) Shrine''' (しりた(お)き[ぎ]じんじゃ), a {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Shikinaisha|WD=Q134917286}} that enshrines Shirita(ogi)-no-Mikoto, one of the princes of {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Mishima Daimyōjin|1=UNKNOWN}} ({{ill|UNKNOWN|WD=Q8009611|lt=Kotoshironushi-no-Kami|1=Kotoshironushi-no-Mikoto}}). Meanwhile, {{ill|lt=Katasuge Shrine (Higashiizu Town)|Q134892414|WD=Q134892414|lt=Katasuge Shrine|1=Katasuge Shrine}} in the neighboring Katase district to the north—thought to have been founded around the same time—also serves as a candidate Shikinaisha enshrining another prince of Mishima Daimyōjin (Kotoshironushi-no-Kami) of Miyakejima, competing with {{ill|lt=Katasuge Shrine|Q134930291|WD=Q134930291|1=Katasuge Shrine}} on Miyakejima itself. Because of this branch-shrine–like relationship, it is possible that these shrines were originally established as refugee branch shrines or distant worship sites relocated from Miyakejima to avoid volcanic eruptions[http://engishiki.org/izu/bun/iz140116-04.html Katasuge Shrine] - Survey of Engishiki Shrines. Although the date of foundation is unknown, a record of reconstruction in the 2nd year of {{ill|lt=Kōei|Q980266|WD=Q980266|1=Kōei}} ({{ill|lt=1343|Q6072|WD=Q6072|1=1343}}) appears in the *Nantō Fudoki*[https://www.town.higashiizu.shizuoka.jp/bg/life/upload/kyo_higashiizutyousi_30mini_6.pdf *History of Higashiizu Town*, Chapter 6, p. 360] - Higashiizu Town. In addition, ridgepole inscriptions from the 2nd year of {{ill|lt=Jōwa|Q1146143|WD=Q1146143|1=Jōwa (Muromachi period)}} ({{ill|lt=1346|Q6080|WD=Q6080|1=1346}}) record the name '''Shirada Ki(hama) Shrine''' (しらだき(はま)じんじゃ) (*Zōtei Tōshū Shikō*). As indicated by its former shrine name and the enshrined deity {{ill|UNKNOWN|WD=Q10881556|lt=Isotakeru-no-Mikoto|1=Isotakeru-no-Mikoto}}, during the Edo period it also strongly exhibited the character of a Kinomiya Shrine associated with {{ill|lt=Kinomiya faith|Q11296898|WD=Q11296898|1=Kinomiya Faith}}. Even today, like {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Kawazu Kinomiya Shrine|1=UNKNOWN}} ({{ill|lt=Sugikubetsu-no-Mikoto Shrine|Q11522170|WD=Q11522170|1=Sugihokowake no Mikoto Shrine}}) in {{ill|lt=Kawazu Town|Q1203861|WD=Q1203861|1=Kawazu, Shizuoka}}, it retains the cultural tradition known as “{{ill|lt=Bird abstinence and sake abstinence of Kawazu|Q11553983|WD=Q11553983|lt=bird abstinence and sake abstinence|1=bird abstinence and sake abstinence}}”On-site shrine history plaque[https://www.at-s.com/facilities/article/view/temple/137787.html Kinomiya Shrine (Higashiizu Town)] - Shizuoka Shimbun. === Enshrined deities === * Shiritaogi-no-Mikoto * {{ill|UNKNOWN|WD=Q10881556|lt=Isotakeru-no-Mikoto|1=Isotakeru-no-Mikoto}} === Notes === {{Reflist}} === External links === * [http://www.shizuoka-jinjacho.or.jp/shokai/jinja.php?id=4401004 Shiritaogi Shrine] - Shizuoka Prefecture Association of Shinto Shrines == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Shiritaogi Shrine]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Shiritaogi Shrine]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shikinai Shōsha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Izu Province]] [[Category:pages]] [[Category:静岡県の神社]] [[Category:東伊豆町の歴史]] [[Category:東伊豆町の建築物]] [[Category:伊豆国の式内社]]

== said to be the same as (P460) == * {{ill|Shiritakino Shrine|lt=Shiritakino Shrine|WD=Q135039656|1=Shiritakino Shrine}} == street address (P6375) == * {'text': '東京都三宅村神着', 'language': 'ja'} [[JA:志理太乎宜神社]] {{wikidata link|Q134892415}} == Japanese content == {{Shrine |Name = Shiritaogi Shrine |Image = {{Image wanted|cat=Kamo District}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=14|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=250|frame-height=250|coord={{coord|34|48|8.3|N|139|3|33.8|E}}}} |Location = {{ill|lt=Shizuoka Prefecture|Q131320|WD=Q131320|1=Shizuoka Prefecture}}{{ill|lt=Kamo District|Q961511|WD=Q961511|1=Kamo District, Shizuoka}}{{ill|lt=Higashiizu Town|Q304491|WD=Q304491|1=Higashiizu}} Shirada 168 |Coordinates = {{Wiki coordinates DMS|34|48|8.3|N|139|3|33.8|E|display=inline,title}} |Enshrined deities = Shiritaogi-no-Mikoto, {{ill|UNKNOWN|WD=Q10881556|lt=Isotakeru-no-Mikoto|1=Isotakeru-no-Mikoto}} |Shrine rank = Former {{ill|lt=village shrine|Q134917284|WD=Q134917284|1=Son-sha}}, candidate {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Shikinaisha|WD=Q134917286}} |Founded = |Main hall = |Other names = Shirada Ki(hama) Shrine, (Shirada) Kinomiya Shrine |Pilgrimage status etc. = |Annual festival = October 24–25 |Rituals = |Map = {{Location map|Japan Shizuoka|label=Shirita
ogi Shrine|coordinates= {{coord2|34|48|8.3|N|139|3|33.8|E|}}|position=left|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} }} '''Shiritaogi Shrine''' (しりたおぎじんじゃ) is a {{ill|lt=Shinto shrine|Q845945|WD=Q845945|1=Shinto shrine}} located in Shirada, {{ill|lt=Higashiizu Town|Q304491|WD=Q304491|1=Higashiizu}}, {{ill|lt=Kamo District|Q961511|WD=Q961511|1=Kamo District, Shizuoka}}, {{ill|lt=Shizuoka Prefecture|Q131320|WD=Q131320|1=Shizuoka Prefecture}}[http://www.shizuoka-jinjacho.or.jp/shokai/jinja.php?id=4401004 Shiritaogi Shrine] - Shizuoka Prefecture Association of Shinto Shrines. During the Edo period it was called '''Kinomiya Shrine''' (きのみやじんじゃ), and this name is still used today as an alternative name, appearing on items such as the plaque on the torii gate[https://kotobank.jp/word/白田村-3062457 What is Shirada Village] - Kotobank[http://engishiki.org/izu/bun/iz140133-02.html Shiritaogi Shrine] - Survey of Engishiki Shrines. === Overview === The shrine is located immediately west of {{ill|lt=Katase-Shirata Station|Q6375267|WD=Q6375267|1=Katase-Shirata Station}}, along {{ill|lt=National Route 135|Q6156779|WD=Q6156779|1=Japan National Route 135}}. Together with {{ill|lt=Shiitore Shrine|Q134930749|WD=Q134930749|1=Shiitori Shrine}} on {{ill|lt=Miyakejima|Q1075076|WD=Q1075076|1=Miyake-jima}}, it is regarded as a candidate shrine (ronsha) of '''Shirita(ogi) Shrine''' (しりた(お)き[ぎ]じんじゃ), a {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Shikinaisha|WD=Q134917286}} that enshrines Shirita(ogi)-no-Mikoto, one of the princes of {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Mishima Daimyōjin|1=UNKNOWN}} ({{ill|UNKNOWN|WD=Q8009611|lt=Kotoshironushi-no-Kami|1=Kotoshironushi-no-Mikoto}}). Meanwhile, {{ill|lt=Katasuge Shrine (Higashiizu Town)|Q134892414|WD=Q134892414|lt=Katasuge Shrine|1=Katasuge Shrine}} in the neighboring Katase district to the north—thought to have been founded around the same time—also serves as a candidate Shikinaisha enshrining another prince of Mishima Daimyōjin (Kotoshironushi-no-Kami) of Miyakejima, competing with {{ill|lt=Katasuge Shrine|Q134930291|WD=Q134930291|1=Katasuge Shrine}} on Miyakejima itself. Because of this branch-shrine–like relationship, it is possible that these shrines were originally established as refugee branch shrines or distant worship sites relocated from Miyakejima to avoid volcanic eruptions[http://engishiki.org/izu/bun/iz140116-04.html Katasuge Shrine] - Survey of Engishiki Shrines. Although the date of foundation is unknown, a record of reconstruction in the 2nd year of {{ill|lt=Kōei|Q980266|WD=Q980266|1=Kōei}} ({{ill|lt=1343|Q6072|WD=Q6072|1=1343}}) appears in the *Nantō Fudoki*[https://www.town.higashiizu.shizuoka.jp/bg/life/upload/kyo_higashiizutyousi_30mini_6.pdf *History of Higashiizu Town*, Chapter 6, p. 360] - Higashiizu Town. In addition, ridgepole inscriptions from the 2nd year of {{ill|lt=Jōwa|Q1146143|WD=Q1146143|1=Jōwa (Muromachi period)}} ({{ill|lt=1346|Q6080|WD=Q6080|1=1346}}) record the name '''Shirada Ki(hama) Shrine''' (しらだき(はま)じんじゃ) (*Zōtei Tōshū Shikō*). As indicated by its former shrine name and the enshrined deity {{ill|UNKNOWN|WD=Q10881556|lt=Isotakeru-no-Mikoto|1=Isotakeru-no-Mikoto}}, during the Edo period it also strongly exhibited the character of a Kinomiya Shrine associated with {{ill|lt=Kinomiya faith|Q11296898|WD=Q11296898|1=Kinomiya Faith}}. Even today, like {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Kawazu Kinomiya Shrine|1=UNKNOWN}} ({{ill|lt=Sugikubetsu-no-Mikoto Shrine|Q11522170|WD=Q11522170|1=Sugihokowake no Mikoto Shrine}}) in {{ill|lt=Kawazu Town|Q1203861|WD=Q1203861|1=Kawazu, Shizuoka}}, it retains the cultural tradition known as “{{ill|lt=Bird abstinence and sake abstinence of Kawazu|Q11553983|WD=Q11553983|lt=bird abstinence and sake abstinence|1=bird abstinence and sake abstinence}}”On-site shrine history plaque[https://www.at-s.com/facilities/article/view/temple/137787.html Kinomiya Shrine (Higashiizu Town)] - Shizuoka Shimbun. === Enshrined deities === * Shiritaogi-no-Mikoto * {{ill|UNKNOWN|WD=Q10881556|lt=Isotakeru-no-Mikoto|1=Isotakeru-no-Mikoto}} === Notes === {{Reflist}} === External links === * [http://www.shizuoka-jinjacho.or.jp/shokai/jinja.php?id=4401004 Shiritaogi Shrine] - Shizuoka Prefecture Association of Shinto Shrines == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Shiritaogi Shrine]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Shiritaogi Shrine]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shikinai Shōsha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Izu Province]] [[Category:pages]] [[Category:静岡県の神社]] [[Category:東伊豆町の歴史]] [[Category:東伊豆町の建築物]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Izu Province]]

== said to be the same as (P460) == * {{ill|Shiritakino Shrine|lt=Shiritakino Shrine|WD=Q135039656|1=Shiritakino Shrine}} == street address (P6375) == * {'text': '東京都三宅村神着', 'language': 'ja'} [[JA:志理太乎宜神社]] {{wikidata link|Q134892415}} == Japanese content == {{Shrine |Name = Shiritaogi Shrine |Image = {{Image wanted|cat=Kamo District}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=14|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=250|frame-height=250|coord={{coord|34|48|8.3|N|139|3|33.8|E}}}} |Location = {{ill|lt=Shizuoka Prefecture|Q131320|WD=Q131320|1=Shizuoka Prefecture}}{{ill|lt=Kamo District|Q961511|WD=Q961511|1=Kamo District, Shizuoka}}{{ill|lt=Higashiizu Town|Q304491|WD=Q304491|1=Higashiizu}} Shirada 168 |Coordinates = {{Wiki coordinates DMS|34|48|8.3|N|139|3|33.8|E|display=inline,title}} |Enshrined deities = Shiritaogi-no-Mikoto, {{ill|UNKNOWN|WD=Q10881556|lt=Isotakeru-no-Mikoto|1=Isotakeru-no-Mikoto}} |Shrine rank = Former {{ill|lt=village shrine|Q134917284|WD=Q134917284|1=Son-sha}}, candidate {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Shikinaisha|WD=Q134917286}} |Founded = |Main hall = |Other names = Shirada Ki(hama) Shrine, (Shirada) Kinomiya Shrine |Pilgrimage status etc. = |Annual festival = October 24–25 |Rituals = |Map = {{Location map|Japan Shizuoka|label=Shirita
ogi Shrine|coordinates= {{coord2|34|48|8.3|N|139|3|33.8|E|}}|position=left|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} }} '''Shiritaogi Shrine''' (しりたおぎじんじゃ) is a {{ill|lt=Shinto shrine|Q845945|WD=Q845945|1=Shinto shrine}} located in Shirada, {{ill|lt=Higashiizu Town|Q304491|WD=Q304491|1=Higashiizu}}, {{ill|lt=Kamo District|Q961511|WD=Q961511|1=Kamo District, Shizuoka}}, {{ill|lt=Shizuoka Prefecture|Q131320|WD=Q131320|1=Shizuoka Prefecture}}[http://www.shizuoka-jinjacho.or.jp/shokai/jinja.php?id=4401004 Shiritaogi Shrine] - Shizuoka Prefecture Association of Shinto Shrines. During the Edo period it was called '''Kinomiya Shrine''' (きのみやじんじゃ), and this name is still used today as an alternative name, appearing on items such as the plaque on the torii gate[https://kotobank.jp/word/白田村-3062457 What is Shirada Village] - Kotobank[http://engishiki.org/izu/bun/iz140133-02.html Shiritaogi Shrine] - Survey of Engishiki Shrines. === Overview === The shrine is located immediately west of {{ill|lt=Katase-Shirata Station|Q6375267|WD=Q6375267|1=Katase-Shirata Station}}, along {{ill|lt=National Route 135|Q6156779|WD=Q6156779|1=Japan National Route 135}}. Together with {{ill|lt=Shiitore Shrine|Q134930749|WD=Q134930749|1=Shiitori Shrine}} on {{ill|lt=Miyakejima|Q1075076|WD=Q1075076|1=Miyake-jima}}, it is regarded as a candidate shrine (ronsha) of '''Shirita(ogi) Shrine''' (しりた(お)き[ぎ]じんじゃ), a {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Shikinaisha|WD=Q134917286}} that enshrines Shirita(ogi)-no-Mikoto, one of the princes of {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Mishima Daimyōjin|1=UNKNOWN}} ({{ill|UNKNOWN|WD=Q8009611|lt=Kotoshironushi-no-Kami|1=Kotoshironushi-no-Mikoto}}). Meanwhile, {{ill|lt=Katasuge Shrine (Higashiizu Town)|Q134892414|WD=Q134892414|lt=Katasuge Shrine|1=Katasuge Shrine}} in the neighboring Katase district to the north—thought to have been founded around the same time—also serves as a candidate Shikinaisha enshrining another prince of Mishima Daimyōjin (Kotoshironushi-no-Kami) of Miyakejima, competing with {{ill|lt=Katasuge Shrine|Q134930291|WD=Q134930291|1=Katasuge Shrine}} on Miyakejima itself. Because of this branch-shrine–like relationship, it is possible that these shrines were originally established as refugee branch shrines or distant worship sites relocated from Miyakejima to avoid volcanic eruptions[http://engishiki.org/izu/bun/iz140116-04.html Katasuge Shrine] - Survey of Engishiki Shrines. Although the date of foundation is unknown, a record of reconstruction in the 2nd year of {{ill|lt=Kōei|Q980266|WD=Q980266|1=Kōei}} ({{ill|lt=1343|Q6072|WD=Q6072|1=1343}}) appears in the *Nantō Fudoki*[https://www.town.higashiizu.shizuoka.jp/bg/life/upload/kyo_higashiizutyousi_30mini_6.pdf *History of Higashiizu Town*, Chapter 6, p. 360] - Higashiizu Town. In addition, ridgepole inscriptions from the 2nd year of {{ill|lt=Jōwa|Q1146143|WD=Q1146143|1=Jōwa (Muromachi period)}} ({{ill|lt=1346|Q6080|WD=Q6080|1=1346}}) record the name '''Shirada Ki(hama) Shrine''' (しらだき(はま)じんじゃ) (*Zōtei Tōshū Shikō*). As indicated by its former shrine name and the enshrined deity {{ill|UNKNOWN|WD=Q10881556|lt=Isotakeru-no-Mikoto|1=Isotakeru-no-Mikoto}}, during the Edo period it also strongly exhibited the character of a Kinomiya Shrine associated with {{ill|lt=Kinomiya faith|Q11296898|WD=Q11296898|1=Kinomiya Faith}}. Even today, like {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Kawazu Kinomiya Shrine|1=UNKNOWN}} ({{ill|lt=Sugikubetsu-no-Mikoto Shrine|Q11522170|WD=Q11522170|1=Sugihokowake no Mikoto Shrine}}) in {{ill|lt=Kawazu Town|Q1203861|WD=Q1203861|1=Kawazu, Shizuoka}}, it retains the cultural tradition known as “{{ill|lt=Bird abstinence and sake abstinence of Kawazu|Q11553983|WD=Q11553983|lt=bird abstinence and sake abstinence|1=bird abstinence and sake abstinence}}”On-site shrine history plaque[https://www.at-s.com/facilities/article/view/temple/137787.html Kinomiya Shrine (Higashiizu Town)] - Shizuoka Shimbun. === Enshrined deities === * Shiritaogi-no-Mikoto * {{ill|UNKNOWN|WD=Q10881556|lt=Isotakeru-no-Mikoto|1=Isotakeru-no-Mikoto}} === Notes === {{Reflist}} === External links === * [http://www.shizuoka-jinjacho.or.jp/shokai/jinja.php?id=4401004 Shiritaogi Shrine] - Shizuoka Prefecture Association of Shinto Shrines == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Shiritaogi Shrine]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Shiritaogi Shrine]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shikinai Shōsha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Izu Province]] [[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Shizuoka Prefecture]] [[Category:東伊豆町の歴史]] [[Category:東伊豆町の建築物]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Izu Province]]

[[JA:志理太乎宜神社]] {{wikidata link|Q134892415}} == Japanese content == {{Shrine |Name = Shiritaogi Shrine |Image = {{Image wanted|cat=Kamo District}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=14|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=250|frame-height=250|coord={{coord|34|48|8.3|N|139|3|33.8|E}}}} |Location = {{ill|lt=Shizuoka Prefecture|Q131320|WD=Q131320|1=Shizuoka Prefecture}}{{ill|lt=Kamo District|Q961511|WD=Q961511|1=Kamo District, Shizuoka}}{{ill|lt=Higashiizu Town|Q304491|WD=Q304491|1=Higashiizu}} Shirada 168 |Coordinates = {{Wiki coordinates DMS|34|48|8.3|N|139|3|33.8|E|display=inline,title}} |Enshrined deities = Shiritaogi-no-Mikoto, {{ill|UNKNOWN|WD=Q10881556|lt=Isotakeru-no-Mikoto|1=Isotakeru-no-Mikoto}} |Shrine rank = Former {{ill|lt=village shrine|Q134917284|WD=Q134917284|1=Son-sha}}, candidate {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Shikinaisha|WD=Q134917286}} |Founded = |Main hall = |Other names = Shirada Ki(hama) Shrine, (Shirada) Kinomiya Shrine |Pilgrimage status etc. = |Annual festival = October 24–25 |Rituals = |Map = {{Location map|Japan Shizuoka|label=Shirita
ogi Shrine|coordinates= {{coord2|34|48|8.3|N|139|3|33.8|E|}}|position=left|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} }} '''Shiritaogi Shrine''' (しりたおぎじんじゃ) is a {{ill|lt=Shinto shrine|Q845945|WD=Q845945|1=Shinto shrine}} located in Shirada, {{ill|lt=Higashiizu Town|Q304491|WD=Q304491|1=Higashiizu}}, {{ill|lt=Kamo District|Q961511|WD=Q961511|1=Kamo District, Shizuoka}}, {{ill|lt=Shizuoka Prefecture|Q131320|WD=Q131320|1=Shizuoka Prefecture}}[http://www.shizuoka-jinjacho.or.jp/shokai/jinja.php?id=4401004 Shiritaogi Shrine] - Shizuoka Prefecture Association of Shinto Shrines. During the Edo period it was called '''Kinomiya Shrine''' (きのみやじんじゃ), and this name is still used today as an alternative name, appearing on items such as the plaque on the torii gate[https://kotobank.jp/word/白田村-3062457 What is Shirada Village] - Kotobank[http://engishiki.org/izu/bun/iz140133-02.html Shiritaogi Shrine] - Survey of Engishiki Shrines. === Overview === The shrine is located immediately west of {{ill|lt=Katase-Shirata Station|Q6375267|WD=Q6375267|1=Katase-Shirata Station}}, along {{ill|lt=National Route 135|Q6156779|WD=Q6156779|1=Japan National Route 135}}. Together with {{ill|lt=Shiitore Shrine|Q134930749|WD=Q134930749|1=Shiitori Shrine}} on {{ill|lt=Miyakejima|Q1075076|WD=Q1075076|1=Miyake-jima}}, it is regarded as a candidate shrine (ronsha) of '''Shirita(ogi) Shrine''' (しりた(お)き[ぎ]じんじゃ), a {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Shikinaisha|WD=Q134917286}} that enshrines Shirita(ogi)-no-Mikoto, one of the princes of {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Mishima Daimyōjin|1=UNKNOWN}} ({{ill|UNKNOWN|WD=Q8009611|lt=Kotoshironushi-no-Kami|1=Kotoshironushi-no-Mikoto}}). Meanwhile, {{ill|lt=Katasuge Shrine (Higashiizu Town)|Q134892414|WD=Q134892414|lt=Katasuge Shrine|1=Katasuge Shrine}} in the neighboring Katase district to the north—thought to have been founded around the same time—also serves as a candidate Shikinaisha enshrining another prince of Mishima Daimyōjin (Kotoshironushi-no-Kami) of Miyakejima, competing with {{ill|lt=Katasuge Shrine|Q134930291|WD=Q134930291|1=Katasuge Shrine}} on Miyakejima itself. Because of this branch-shrine–like relationship, it is possible that these shrines were originally established as refugee branch shrines or distant worship sites relocated from Miyakejima to avoid volcanic eruptions[http://engishiki.org/izu/bun/iz140116-04.html Katasuge Shrine] - Survey of Engishiki Shrines. Although the date of foundation is unknown, a record of reconstruction in the 2nd year of {{ill|lt=Kōei|Q980266|WD=Q980266|1=Kōei}} ({{ill|lt=1343|Q6072|WD=Q6072|1=1343}}) appears in the *Nantō Fudoki*[https://www.town.higashiizu.shizuoka.jp/bg/life/upload/kyo_higashiizutyousi_30mini_6.pdf *History of Higashiizu Town*, Chapter 6, p. 360] - Higashiizu Town. In addition, ridgepole inscriptions from the 2nd year of {{ill|lt=Jōwa|Q1146143|WD=Q1146143|1=Jōwa (Muromachi period)}} ({{ill|lt=1346|Q6080|WD=Q6080|1=1346}}) record the name '''Shirada Ki(hama) Shrine''' (しらだき(はま)じんじゃ) (*Zōtei Tōshū Shikō*). As indicated by its former shrine name and the enshrined deity {{ill|UNKNOWN|WD=Q10881556|lt=Isotakeru-no-Mikoto|1=Isotakeru-no-Mikoto}}, during the Edo period it also strongly exhibited the character of a Kinomiya Shrine associated with {{ill|lt=Kinomiya faith|Q11296898|WD=Q11296898|1=Kinomiya Faith}}. Even today, like {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Kawazu Kinomiya Shrine|1=UNKNOWN}} ({{ill|lt=Sugikubetsu-no-Mikoto Shrine|Q11522170|WD=Q11522170|1=Sugihokowake no Mikoto Shrine}}) in {{ill|lt=Kawazu Town|Q1203861|WD=Q1203861|1=Kawazu, Shizuoka}}, it retains the cultural tradition known as “{{ill|lt=Bird abstinence and sake abstinence of Kawazu|Q11553983|WD=Q11553983|lt=bird abstinence and sake abstinence|1=bird abstinence and sake abstinence}}”On-site shrine history plaque[https://www.at-s.com/facilities/article/view/temple/137787.html Kinomiya Shrine (Higashiizu Town)] - Shizuoka Shimbun. === Enshrined deities === * Shiritaogi-no-Mikoto * {{ill|UNKNOWN|WD=Q10881556|lt=Isotakeru-no-Mikoto|1=Isotakeru-no-Mikoto}} === Notes === {{Reflist}} === External links === * [http://www.shizuoka-jinjacho.or.jp/shokai/jinja.php?id=4401004 Shiritaogi Shrine] - Shizuoka Prefecture Association of Shinto Shrines == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Shiritaogi Shrine]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Shiritaogi Shrine]][[Category:Shikinai Shōsha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Izu Province]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Shizuoka Prefecture]] [[Category:東伊豆町の歴史]] [[Category:東伊豆町の建築物]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Izu Province]]
